news.Title: Tesla dodges more headaches as autonomous driving scores a win
news.Content:
Tesla scored a rare victory with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) on Monday after the Office of Defects and Investigations decided to close its investigation into its Actually Smart Summon tech, which allows drivers to summon their parked vehicles to them using just their phones.

The ODI opened an investigation into multiple reported crashes on January 6, 2025, and, after analyzing complaint data provided by Tesla and consumer complaints, the agency concluded that, since most of the reported crashes involved minor property damage and no serious injuries, it was ending its inquiry.

More Tesla

Tesla loses crucial Autopilot ruling that could cost hundreds of millions

New Tesla vision sounds almost too good to be true

Tesla proves it truly is a tech (not car) company with latest move

The NHTSA has been busy auditing Tesla lately , including opening an "engineering analysis" into the company last month over its other autonomous driving tech, Full Self-Driving (Supervised).

Autonomous driving in general is a hot topic as thousands of them operate in major cities like San Francisco, Austin, and Miami every day.

The NHTSA opened a Preliminary Evaluation in October to investigate an estimated 2,000 Waymo 5th-gen automated driving system-equipped vehicles, following a Georgia media report that revealed the company's vehicles did not stop for crossing schoolchildren despite repeated assertions that it had fixed the issue .

While those are different levels of autonomy from the investigation that just ended with Tesla, the technology is built on the same principles, and just because no one has been injured by Actually Smart Summon yet, it doesn't mean they won't be in the future.

NHTSA clears Tesla autonomous tech because of the lack of injuries, so far

On Monday, NHTSA announced that it is closing its investigation into Tesla's Actually Smart Summon tech, which acts as a short-distance autonomous system, after more than a year.

The agency says that out of millions of sessions, only a fraction of 1% resulted in an adverse incident, and none of them were fatal.

"Almost all those incidents took place where, typically early in a Summon session, the system or person using the app failed to fully detect or respond appropriately to vehicle surroundings, resulting in minor impacts. Incidents took place when app users did not have a complete 360-degree view of the surroundings in the app to assess situational awareness," ODI said.

"This limited the app userвҖҷs ability to determine whether an impact was imminent during initial vehicle maneuvers such as reversing in close proximity to an obstacle or a curb. ODI found that the impacts most often occurred with parking gates, adjacently parked vehicles, and short parking bollards."

Story Continues

Related: Rivian defies expectations despite rough EV environment

Two of the crashes ODI investigated were related to camera blockages. In both cases, the vehicles were trying to navigate a snowy parking lot "with snow partially or fully obstructing the forward-facing cameras."

The technology didn't detect the camera blockage, and the vehicles collided with unoccupied parked vehicles.

Tesla issued an over-the-air software update to implement camera blockage detection, so users can know when the vehicle is in a blind spot; however, another recent investigation suggests that Tesla's camera blockage issue is recurring.

NHTSA opens вҖҳengineering analysisвҖҷ into Tesla FSD cameras

In March, the ODI announced it was escalating its investigation into Tesla FSD, opening an вҖңengineering analysisвҖқ to evaluate Tesla VisionвҖҷs вҖңdegradation detection system.вҖқ

The NHTSA is investigating how much camera visibility is degraded by roadway conditions such as glare and airborne obstructions, and whether Tesla FSD (Supervised) can detect and adjust to the resulting degradation to still work safely.

вҖңAvailable incident data raise concerns that TeslaвҖҷs degradation detection system, both as originally deployed and later updated, fails to detect and/or warn the driver appropriately under degraded visibility conditions,вҖқ the NHTSA said.

The agency has identified nine crashes in which it says Tesla FSDвҖҷs degradation systems may not have been functioning properly. It says FSD вҖңdid not detect common roadway conditions that impaired its visibility and/or provide alerts when camera performance had deteriorated until immediately before the crash occurred.вҖқ

And there could be many more instances that the agency does not know about because their review of TeslaвҖҷs responses to its request for more information revealed вҖңadditional crashes that occurred in similar environments and where the system either did not detect a degraded state, and/or it did not present the driver with an alert with adequate time for the driver to react.вҖқ

In each of those crashes, FSD lost track of or failed to detect a lead vehicle. Still, ODI says, Tesla has described вҖңinternal data and labeling limitationsвҖқ that have prevented it from being sure it has caught every crash due to degraded vision. So there could be more crash incidents than are currently known.

Tesla did not respond to a request for comment.

Tesla says it does not need LiDAR

While the NHTSA directly lists the lack of radar as a possible component in these crashes, Tesla says the system is unnecessary.

Most experts consider a light detection and ranging driver-assistance system to be the state-of-the-art technology. Tesla competitors like Toyota offer LiDAR, in addition to the camera-based system that Tesla FSD uses.

But Musk has called LiDAR an вҖңexpensive and unnecessaryвҖқ foolвҖҷs errand , just вҖңexpensive hardware thatвҖҷs worthless on the car.вҖқ

Recently, Tesla said that its FSD system has driven a cumulative total of 3.6 billion miles, nearly triple the 1.3 billion miles it reported a year ago.

Related: Tesla LiDAR stance accelerates NHTSA investigation into FSD

This story was originally published by TheStreet on Apr 7, 2026, where it first appeared in the Automotive section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

news.Title: Could 2026 Be The Year TeslaвҖҷs (TSLA) Foreign Sales Recover?
news.Content:
Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA ) is one of the 8 Best American Stocks to Buy for the Next 5 Years . On April 2, Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) reported that its China-made electric vehicle sales continued to grow for a second straight quarter, even amid growing competition and as the company expands its focus beyond EVs. According to data from the China Passenger Car Association, the companyвҖҷs Shanghai factory sold 85,670 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles in March. This total, which includes units exported to Europe and other markets, represents a 8.7% year-over-year increase. This marks five consecutive months of growth, supported by recovering European demand.

A Tesla car. Photo from Tesla website

For the first quarter of 2026, China-made sales grew 23.5% compared to the last year, up from a 1.9% rise in Q4 FY 2025. Analysts said Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and other EV makers could also benefit from higher oil prices following the Iran crisis.

Globally, TSLAвҖҷs first-quarter deliveries are expected to rebound nearly 10% from a year earlier, when some consumers showed reluctance over CEO Elon MuskвҖҷs political views. Competition in the EV market remains strong, especially from Chinese manufacturers. Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) lost nearly half of its European market share last year. In China, the companyвҖҷs share of the EV market declined from 10% in 2024 to 8%.

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) is a developer, manufacturer, designer, lessor, and seller of electric vehicles and energy generation and storage systems. The company operates across China, the United States, and globally. It operates through the Automotive and Energy Generation and Storage segments.

While we acknowledge the potential of TSLA as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If youвҖҷre looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock .

news.Title: Should You Invest in SIMO Stock on Rising Estimate Revisions?
news.Content:
Silicon Motion Technology Corporation SIMO is currently witnessing an uptrend in estimate revisions. Earnings estimates for SIMO for 2026 have moved up 36.9% to $5.75 over the past year, while the same for 2027 has increased 50.6% to $7.77. The positive estimate revision portrays bullish sentiments about the stockвҖҷs growth potential.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

What Propels SIMO?

Silicon Motion is benefiting from being a leading merchant supplier of client SSD (solid state drive) controllers to module makers, including most market leaders in the United States, Taiwan and China. The company believes it is well-equipped to adapt to industry changes, as it has collaborated with flash vendors to develop proprietary controller technology to address the existing weaknesses of 3D NAND and outshine peers. Silicon Motion has commenced initial sales of 3D SSD controllers to flash partners. It expects this controller to be a significant driver of SSD controller growth over the next year, as NAND Flash partnersвҖҷ 3D capacity expands.

The company commenced mass production of PCIe NVMe client SSD controllers for flash partners. Silicon Motion has also rolled out the world's first PCIe Gen5 client SSD controller, SM2508, leveraging TSMC's 6nm EUV process. This cutting-edge controller is capable of achieving 50% lower power consumption compared to 12nm counterparts, offering up to 1.7x better power efficiency than PCIe Gen4 SSDs.

Silicon Motion has expanded its SSD controller program engagements with PC OEMs and eMMC/UFS controllers for smartphones, automotive applications and IoT/smart devices. The company is adding to this momentum with the upcoming launch of its next-generation enterprise-class SSD controllers. Silicon MotionвҖҷs eMMC is showing strong signals of rebound, thereby adding to the strength of its overall embedded storage market that comprises both SSD controllers and eMMC embedded memory. As market trends suggest the balance is tilting from transitioning of eMMC 4.5 toward that of eMMC 5.0, the company foresees lucrative prospects for eMMC 5.1 controller sales.

SIMO Rides on Fabless Business Model

Silicon Motion operates a fabless business model, focusing on chip design while outsourcing manufacturing to foundries like TSMC. Consequently, the company has a low capital investment requirement as it does not require expensive fabrication plants, enabling it to adopt advanced manufacturing nodes quickly, leading to higher margins compared to integrated manufacturers. This enables the company to focus on innovation and product development rather than manufacturing complexity.

The key growth drivers for SIMO include AI and high-performance computing, cloud data centers, automotive storage, smartphones and mobile devices. Each of these end markets is growing fast and offers lucrative growth potential. We believe an expanding customer base and innovative products will act as tailwinds for the companyвҖҷs top-line growth, going forward. Over the last 10 years, Silicon Motion has shipped more than 5 billion controllers cumulatively вҖ“ more than any other company in the world. Silicon Motion ships more than 750 million NAND controllers on average every year.

Story Continues

Price Performance

SIMO has gained a stellar 199.1% over the past year compared with the industryвҖҷs growth of 145.5%. It has also outperformed peers like Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. AMD and International Business Machines Corporation IBM. Advanced Micro has gained 181.5% and IBM is up 11.6% over this period.

One-Year SIMO Stock Price Performance

Stiff Competition, Geopolitical Issues Hurt SIMO

Silicon Motion faces stiff competition in the mobile storage market from players like Micron, Microchip, Marvell and ON Semiconductor. Of late, intensifying competition in the card and USB flash drive controller markets is proving to be a major headwind. The tense political condition between Taiwan and the PeopleвҖҷs Republic of China increases the extent of political risk for the company. Moreover, North KoreaвҖҷs nuclear and ballistic missile testing programs, deteriorating relationship with South Korea, leadership woes and strained relations with the United States are likely to affect its business. In addition, high R&D costs have adversely impacted its margins.

End Note

With solid fundamentals and healthy revenue-generating potential, driven by robust demand trends, Silicon Motion is poised for long-term growth. Further, a strong emphasis on quality, diligent execution of operational plans and continuous portfolio enhancements are driving more value for customers. An asset-light fabless semiconductor model, solid growth exposure to AI, cloud and automotive markets, with increasing market share in SSD and mobile controllers and continuous innovation in storage technologies are key growth drivers for the company. With upward earnings estimate revisions, the stock is witnessing positive investor sentiment.

However, stiff competition and supply chain issues are likely to put pressure on the bottom-line growth. High R&D costs erode its profitability to a large extent. Silicon Motion is facing a tough operating environment amid tense geopolitical conditions across the globe. With a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), Silicon Motion appears to be treading in the middle of the road, and investors could be better off if they trade with caution. You can see the complete list of todayвҖҷs Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: Retirees: Here's How to Ride Out the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Sell-Off Without Wrecking Your Portfolio
news.Content: Financial headlines feel personal when you're retired. news.Title: ASML's Installed Base Business Gains Traction: What's Ahead?
news.Content:
ASML Holding вҖҷs ASML upgrade and service business has been a strong contributor to its Installed Base Management (IBM) segment throughout the past year. In 2025, ASMLвҖҷs IBM sales increased 26% to EUR 8.2 billion, driven by higher service and upgrades of its wafer fabrication equipment business.

In the first half of 2025, ASMLвҖҷs IBM strength was more upgrade-driven and in the second half of 2025, toward recurring service revenues. ASML made strong progress in completing a growing number of field upgrades of NXE:3800E systems with a 220-wafers-per-hour configuration.

ASML HoldingвҖҷs expanding Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) Lithography installed base is increasingly driving service demand. The company is moving into sub-2nm production with High Numerical Aperture (High-NA) EUV systems, ensuring further growth. Given these factors, the IBM business is expected to contribute to a quarter of the top line for ASML.

For the first quarter of 2026, ASML expects its IBM sales to be around EUR 2.4 billion, mainly driven by the growing installed base of EUV systems and ASMLвҖҷs customers' plans to upgrade the performance to support the scaling of their capacity. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for ASMLвҖҷs 2026 revenues indicates growth of 20% year over year.

How Competitors Fare Against ASML

ASML Holding is effectively the sole player in lithography system upgrades for its own installed base, especially in EUV space. However, in the broader semiconductor equipment market, ASML competes with companies like Lam Research LRCX and Applied Materials AMAT.

Lam Research is an established wafer fabrication equipment manufacturer that is established in the memory space. Lam ResearchвҖҷs memory segment, accounting for both Dynamic Random Access Memory and Non-Volatile Memory divisions, is gaining traction on the back of AI.

Applied Materials supplies equipment used in chip fabrication, including deposition and etching tools that are essential for both advanced and mature nodes. As chips become more complex with AI and high-performance workloads, Applied MaterialsвҖҷ tools aid in designing and making efficient and smaller node chips.

ASMLвҖҷs Price Performance, Valuation and Estimates

Shares of ASML have surged 119% in the past 12 months compared with the Zacks Computer and Technology sectorвҖҷs growth of 52.4%.

ASML 12-Month Performance Chart

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

From a valuation standpoint, ASML trades at a forward price-to-sales ratio of 11.12X, higher than the sectorвҖҷs average of 5.18X.

ASML Forward 12 Month (P/S) Valuation Chart

Story Continues

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for ASMLвҖҷs 2026 and 2027 earnings implies year-over-year growth of 20% and 23%, respectively. The consensus estimate for fiscal 2026 has been revised downward in the past seven days.

ASML currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of todayвҖҷs Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: Tesla's Business Has Become Much More Diversified in Just the Past Five Years. Does That Make Its Stock a Better Buy Today?
news.Content: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is known for its electric vehicles (EVs), and while they are still its bread and butter, the company has been evolving and diversifying its operations over the years. That can make it less susceptible to market conditions and growing competition. Here's a look at how much more diversified Tesla's business has become in recent years, and whether that could make it a better growth stock to own in the long run. Will AI create ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Tesla's business has diversified, with non-automotive revenue accounting for over 27% of its $94.8 billion total revenue in 2025.
вҖў Automotive revenue declined by 10%, while services grew 19% and energy generation/storage increased sales by 27%.
вҖў Despite diversification, Tesla's gross profit margin fell to 18% from 25% in 2021.
вҖў The stock remains expensive with a P/E ratio of over 300 times earnings.
вҖў Intensifying competition in the EV market and low margins make it difficult to justify the high valuation.
news.Keywords: business diversification, non-automotive revenue, gross margin, valuation, EV market, competition

news.Title: Coherent Soars 379% in a Year: Buy, Sell, or Hold the Stock?
news.Content:
Coherent Corp. вҖҷs COHR stock has skyrocketed 379.3% over the past year, outperforming the industry's 29.4% growth and the Zacks S&P 500 CompositeвҖҷs 35.6% rally.

1-Year Share Price Performance

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Let us analyze this stock to find out whether you should ride the rally or stay away from it.

CoherentвҖҷs Goldmine: Segment Growth & Diverse Product Line

COHRвҖҷs datacenter & communications segment generated 72% of the top line during the second quarter of fiscal 2026, a hike from the year-ago quarterвҖҷs 63%. With the rising proportion of the top line, this segment witnessed year-over-year revenue growth of 33.5%. This outstanding performance can be attributed to the surging demand for 800-gig and 1.6T transceivers.

The datacenter & communications segment was the vital cog in the companyвҖҷs growth engine. However, the industrial business recovery must be acknowledged. The company witnessed high demand on the back of strong orders from semi-cap equipment customers, indicating solid growth in the industrial business. This segmentвҖҷs revenues moved up 4% sequentially, led by industrial laser and engineered material product lines.

COHR holds an array of products, which, when combined with vertical integration and U.S. manufacturing expansion, yield a strategic advantage. In the second quarter of fiscal 2026, growth was not solely dependent on the rising demand for transceivers but also on Optical Circuit Switch Systems. Coherent successfully scaled its Indium Phosphide capacity actively, ramping the 6-inch wafer production across Sherman and Jarfalla, resulting in a strategic shift from an industrial focus to becoming a vital player in the AI revolution.

COHRвҖҷs Balance Sheet: Key to Outpace Competitors

Coherent ended the second quarter of fiscal 2026 with $899 million in cash reserves, an improvement from the preceding quarterвҖҷs $875 million. This cash chest stands against a current debt of $106 million as of the end of second-quarter fiscal 2026, a clear indication of solid liquidity. It is solidified by the fact that COHRвҖҷs current ratio is at 2.25, which is significantly higher than the industry average of 1.57.

The company managed to maintain a long-term debt of $3.2 billion as of the end of December 2025, flat with the preceding quarterвҖҷs actual. The proportion of total debt is at 26.8% of the total capital, which is lower than the preceding quarterвҖҷs 34.9%, as well as the industry average of 35.4%. This evident reduction in financial risks is further solidified by the fact that timed interest earned climbed up to 2.5X from the preceding quarterвҖҷs 2.2X, hinting at an ease in meeting interest obligations.

Story Continues

This strong balance sheet position fueled shareholder value. Over the past six months, COHR experienced explosive growth of 117% against the industryвҖҷs 21.2% dip. CoherentвҖҷs competitors Wolfspeed WOLF and ON Semiconductor ON did not show the same movement. While ON Semiconductor has gained 24.7%, Wolfspeed has lost 40.6%. While Coherent pivoted to AI connectivity, Wolfspeed and ON Semiconductor locked horns in the EV powertrain.

6-Month Share Price Performance

CoherentвҖҷs Robust Top & Bottom-Line Prospects

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for COHRвҖҷs fiscal 2026 revenues is pinned at $6.9 billion, indicating a 19.4% year-over-year rise. For fiscal 2027, the same is expected to gain 23.2% from the year-ago quarterвҖҷs actual. The consensus mark for fiscal 2026 EPS is set at $5.39, indicating 52.7% year-over-year growth. For fiscal 2027, the bottom line is anticipated to rise 35.4%.

Over the past 60 days, eight and seven EPS estimates for fiscal 2026 and 2027 have been revised upward, respectively, with no downward adjustments. During the same period, the Zacks Consensus Estimate for fiscal 2026 earnings has increased 5.5%, and for fiscal 2027, it has risen 13.2%, demonstrating analyst confidence.

COHR Trades Pricier Than Industry

Coherent is priced at 36.94 times forward 12-month earnings per share, higher than the industry average of 19.8 times. Similarly, the companyвҖҷs trailing 12-month EV-to-EBITDA ratio is 36.15 times, trading at a premium compared with the industry average of 14.59 times. These metrics validate CoherentвҖҷs overvaluation, a red flag for investors.

CoherentвҖҷs Reluctance to Pay Out Dividends Is a Red Flag

COHR does not have any plan to pay out cash dividends. Hence, the only way to reap the benefits of return on investment on the companyвҖҷs stock is through share price appreciation, which is not guaranteed. Such moves demotivate investors seeking cash dividends.

Conclusion: Hold COHR Stock Now

CoherentвҖҷs improving segmental performance, coupled with the rising demand for its diverse product line and a solid balance sheet, paves a solid long-term foundation. However, the current valuation is a significant obstacle. Trading nearly double that of the industry average amid such an explosive rally in its stock price raises questions about its continuance.

The lack of dividends becomes another waving red flag for income-seeking investors. Weighing the pros and cons, we recommend shareholders retain this stock for now. Potential buyers are urged to take a cautious approach and look for technical corrections before buying into this AI powerhouse.

COHR currently has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of todayвҖҷs Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

news.Title: Stocks Tumble and Crude Prices Jump on Fears of Iran War Escalation
news.Content:
The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) today is down -0.63%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) is down -0.66%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) is down -0.78%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) are down -0.60%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) are down -0.69%.

Stock indexes are slipping today as crude oil prices climbed more than +2% to a 4-week high ahead of tonightвҖҷs deadline for Iran to agree to a ceasefire or face escalation of the war. The markets are watching for any sign of a breakthrough amid a flurry of diplomacy before President TrumpвҖҷs 8 pm EST deadline tonight. Mr. Trump insists any deal must ensure uninterrupted transit through the Strait of Hormuz or he will destroy IranвҖҷs bridges and power plants. The Wall Street Journal reported the US military is making preparations for potential strikes on energy targets in Iran.

More News from Barchart

NikeвҖҷs Real Problem IsnвҖҷt Sales: ItвҖҷs Relevance

Why Are ORCL Stock Investors So Excited About Oracle Layoffs?

Dear Costco Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for May 1

Get exclusive insights with the FREE Barchart Brief newsletter. Subscribe now for quick, incisive midday market analysis you won't find anywhere else.

Crude oil prices (CLK26) remain volatile, fluctuating between gains and losses amid news headlines about Iran. Crude is up more than +2% today after Axios reported that the US conducted strikes on military targets on Kharg Island today and Israel told Iranians to refrain from using their countryвҖҷs railway network. Also, Iran pressed on with attacks across the Persian Gulf, dimming chances for peace.

TodayвҖҷs US economic news was supportive for stocks after Feb capital goods new orders nondefense ex-aircraft and parts, a proxy for capital spending, rose +0.6% m/m, stronger than expectations of +0.5% m/m.

Dovish comments today from New York Fed President John Williams were supportive of stocks, as he said the outlook for underlying price pressures in the US was largely unchanged, despite his expectation that higher energy costs stemming from the war in Iran will boost overall inflation. He added that he anticipated core inflation would rise by just one or two tenths of a percentage point.

The markets are discounting a 3% chance for a +25 bp FOMC rate hike at the April 28-29 policy meeting.

Overseas stock markets are mixed today. The Euro Stoxx 50 is down -0.249%. China's Shanghai Composite closed up +0.26%. Japan's Nikkei Stock 225 closed up +0.03%.

Story Continues

Interest Rates

June 10-year T-notes (ZNM6) today are unchanged. The 10-year T-note yield is unchanged at 4.331%. T-notes prices recovered from early losses and are little changed after dovish comments from New York Fed President John Williams, who said the outlook for underlying price pressures in the US was largely unchanged, despite his expectations of higher energy costs stemming from the war in Iran. Also, safe-haven demand for T-notes has increased ahead of President TrumpвҖҷs deadline of tonight for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

TodayвҖҷs rally in WTI crude oil by more than +2% to a 4-week high is boosting inflation expectations and is negative for T-notes. Also, supply pressures are weighing on T-notes as the Treasury will auction $119 billion of T-notes and T-bonds this week, beginning with todayвҖҷs $58 billion auction of 3-year T-notes.

European government bond yields are moving higher today. The 10-year German bund yield is up +4.6 bp to 3.038%. The 10-year UK gilt yield is up +4.4 bp to 4.876%.

The Eurozone Apr Sentix investor confidence index fell -16.1 to a 2.5-year low of -19.2, weaker than expectations of -8.0.

The Eurozone Mar S&P composite PMI was revised upward by +0.2 to 50.7 from the previously reported 50.5.

ECB Governing Council member Pierre Wunsch said the ECB might have to increase interest rates several times if the Iran war drags on.

The UK Mar S&P services PMI was revised downward by -0.7 to an 11-month low of 50.5 from the previously reported 51.2.

Swaps are discounting a 58% chance of a +25 bp ECB rate hike at its next policy meeting on April 30.

US Stock Movers

Airline stocks and cruise line operators are falling today, with crude oil prices up more than +2% at a 4-week high, raising fuel costs and cutting into corporate profits. Carnival (CCL), Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), and Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd (RCL) are down more than -3%, and United Airlines Holdings (UAL) is down more than -2%. Also, American Airlines Group (AAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV), Alaska Air Group (ALK), and Delta Air Lines (DAL) are down more than -1%.

Cryptocurrency-exposed stocks are moving lower today, with Bitcoin (^BTCUSD) down by more than -2%. Coinbase Global (COIN), MARA Holdings (MARA), and Galaxy Digital Holdings (GLXY) are down more than -2%. Also, Strategy (MSTR) is down more than -1%, and Riot Platforms (RIOT) is down -0.22%.

Homebuilders are sliding today after Seaport Global Securities downgraded the sector. Lennar (LEN), KB Home (KBH), and PulteGroup (PHM) are down more than -2% after being double-downgraded to sell from buy. DR Horton (DHI) and Toll Brothers (TOL) are also down more than -2% after Seaport Global Securities downgraded the stocks to neutral from buy.

Managed care stocks are climbing today after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services finalized a 2.48% rate hike for health insurers in 2027. UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is up more than +7% to lead gainers in the Dow Jones Industrials, and Humana (HUM) and CVS Health (CVS) are up more than +5%. Also, Elevance Health (ELV) is up more than +3%, Centene (CNC) is up more than +2%, and Molina Healthcare (MOH) is up +0.35%.

ARM Holdings Plc (ARM) is down more than -4% to lead losers in the Nasdaq 100 after Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock to equal weight from overweight.

Apple (AAPL) is down more than -3% to lead losers in the Dow Jones industrials after Nikkei Asia reported the company is seeing setbacks in the engineering test phase of its foldable iPhone that could push back its production and shipment schedule.

Tractor Supply Co (TSCO) is down more than -3% after Bank of America Global Research downgraded the stock to neutral from buy.

First Interstate BancSystem (FIBK) is down more than -2% after UBS downgraded the stock to sell from neutral with a price target of $30.

ASML Holding NV (ASML) is down by more than 1% after US lawmakers unveiled legislation aimed at tightening restrictions on chip-tool exports to China.

Phillips 66 (PSX) is down more than -1% after saying its Q1 results were impacted by about $900 million in pre-tax mark-to-market losses due to increases in commodity prices impacting its short positions.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) is up more than +12% to lead gainers in the S&P 500, and it entered into equity syndication agreements with a vehicle of the Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund LвҖҷimad Holding to support its acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery.

FuboTV (FUBO) is up more than +5% after Barrington Research upgraded the stock to outperform from market perform with a price target of $16.

CF Industries Holdings (CF) is up more than +4% after RBC Capital Markets boosted its price target on the stock to $125 from $100.

Broadcom (AVGO) is up more than +3% to lead gainers in the Nasdaq 100 after announcing a long-term agreement with Google to develop and supply Tensor Processing Units and confirming plans to power some of AnthropicвҖҷs operations.

news.Content:

и©ІиЁҲз•«иў«иҰ–зӮәжү“йҖ жңӘдҫҶ AI з®—еҠӣеҹәзӨҺзҡ„йҮҚиҰҒеёғеұҖпјҢй•·жңҹзӣ®жЁҷжӣҙзһ„жә–иҲҮеҸ°з©Қйӣ» (TSM-US)(2330-TW) зӯүй ҳе…Ҳжҷ¶зүҮд»Је·Ҙе» з«¶зҲӯгҖӮ

иӢұзү№зҲҫиЎЁзӨәпјҢе°ҮжҶ‘и—үе…¶еңЁжҷ¶зүҮиЁӯиЁҲгҖҒиЈҪйҖ иҲҮе°ҒиЈқж–№йқўзҡ„иғҪеҠӣпјҢдёҰеҚ”еҠ©йҮҚж§ӢпјҲrefactorпјүжҷ¶зүҮиЈҪйҖ жҠҖиЎ“жһ¶ж§ӢпјҢеҠ йҖҹ Terafab йҮҸз”ўйҖІзЁӢпјҢжҺЁйҖІжҜҸе№ҙйҒ”еҲ° 1 еӨӘз“ҰпјҲterawattпјүйҒӢз®—иғҪеҠӣзҡ„йЎҳжҷҜпјҢд»Ҙж”ҜжҸҙ AI иҲҮж©ҹеҷЁдәәй ҳеҹҹзҡ„й•·жңҹзҷјеұ•гҖӮиӢұзү№зҲҫеҹ·иЎҢй•·йҷізҰҸйҷҪпјҲLip-Bu TanпјүиЎЁзӨәпјҢTerafab д»ЈиЎЁжңӘдҫҶжҷ¶зүҮйӮҸијҜгҖҒиЁҳжҶ¶й«”иҲҮе°ҒиЈқиЈҪйҖ ж–№ејҸзҡ„гҖҢйҮҚеӨ§иҪүи®ҠгҖҚпјҢе…¬еҸёе°ҮиҲҮйҰ¬ж–Ҝе…ӢеҜҶеҲҮеҗҲдҪңжҺЁеӢ•и©Ій …жҲ°з•ҘиЁҲз•«гҖӮ

еёӮе ҙе°ҚжӯӨж¶ҲжҒҜеҸҚжҮүжӯЈйқўпјҢиӢұзү№зҲҫиӮЎеғ№еңЁе‘ЁдәҢзӣӨеҲқдёҖеәҰдёҠжјІзҙ„ 4.9%пјҢйҡЁеҫҢжјІе№…ж”¶ж–ӮпјҢд»Қз¶ӯжҢҒдёҠжҸҡиө°еӢўпјҢе»¶зәҢд»Ҡе№ҙд»ҘдҫҶзҡ„еј·еӢўиЎЁзҸҫпјҢзҙҜиЁҲжјІе№…е·ІйҒ”зҙ„ 38%гҖӮжӯӨеӨ–пјҢSpaceX иҝ‘жңҹе·ІдҪҺиӘҝз”іи«ӢеңЁзҫҺеңӢйҖІиЎҢйҰ–ж¬Ўе…¬й–ӢеӢҹиӮЎпјҲIPOпјүпјҢдёҰиЁҲз•«ж–јд»Ҡе№ҙзЁҚжҷҡе•ҹеӢ•дёҠеёӮпјҢйҖІдёҖжӯҘжҸҗеҚҮеёӮе ҙе°ҚйҰ¬ж–Ҝе…Ӣ AI з”ҹж…Ӣзі»еёғеұҖзҡ„й—ңжіЁгҖӮ

ж•ҙй«”иҖҢиЁҖпјҢйҡЁи‘—з§‘жҠҖе·Ёй ӯеҠ зўјжҠ•иіҮ AI еҹәзӨҺе»әиЁӯпјҢжҷ¶зүҮиЈҪйҖ иҲҮз®—еҠӣз«¶иіҪжҢҒзәҢеҚҮжә«пјҢиӢұзү№зҲҫеҠ е…Ҙ Terafab иЁҲз•«пјҢдёҚеғ…еј·еҢ–е…¶еңЁ AI дҫӣжҮүйҸҲдёӯзҡ„и§’иүІпјҢд№ҹеҮёйЎҜйҰ¬ж–Ҝе…ӢжӯЈеҠ йҖҹжү“йҖ еһӮзӣҙж•ҙеҗҲзҡ„жҷ¶зүҮиЈҪйҖ иғҪеҠӣпјҢи©Ұең–еңЁжңӘдҫҶз®—еҠӣз«¶иіҪдёӯеҚ ж“ҡй—ңйҚөдҪҚзҪ®гҖӮ

news.Title: Intel Could Score Major Packaging Customers. Should You Buy INTC Stock Now?
news.Content:
Intel (INTC) has been derided for squandering its leadership position in the chip race while also not leveraging its capabilities fully as a full-stack provider of everything semiconductors. No wonder it is languishing behind peers like Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO).

However, under the leadership of industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan, Intel is finally looking to regain its mojo. While the 18A chip and the strategic move to separate the design and foundry businesses can be long-term triggers if played out correctly, the company's packaging business is also gaining traction.

More News from Barchart

NikeвҖҷs Real Problem IsnвҖҷt Sales: ItвҖҷs Relevance

Why Are ORCL Stock Investors So Excited About Oracle Layoffs?

Dear Costco Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for May 1

Stop Missing Market Moves: Get the FREE Barchart Brief вҖ“ your midday dose of stock movers, trending sectors, and actionable trade ideas, delivered right to your inbox. Sign Up Now!

With partners like Microsoft (MSFT) and Cisco (CSCO) already on board, media reports have emerged that Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) are also mulling onboarding Intel as one of their advanced packaging partners. INTC stock went up marginally on the news, but it is already up 41% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis.

Thus, can this provide another leg up for INTC stock this year? Let's find out.

Advanced Packaging: Ace up the Sleeve

The advanced chip packaging market is a growing and lucrative one, with forecasts of the same reaching $55 billion by 2030. Although TSMC (TSM) is the undisputed leader with a market share of more than 60%, Intel is slowly but surely carving out its place in this space.

Currently, Intel captures just a high single-digit share of the outsourced advanced packaging market, though this figure is expanding rapidly. The massive total addressable market allows the company to secure billions in new revenue simply by acting as a secondary supplier for tech giants trying to avoid Asian supply chain bottlenecks.

What works in favor of Intel is that the company possesses a unique structural advantage by operating as an integrated systems foundry right on American soil. Competitors like Amkor (AMKR) and ASE excel at outsourced assembly but cannot manufacture the underlying silicon wafers. Then, compared to TSMC and Samsung, Intel differentiates itself through geographic security and specialized interconnect technologies.

Notably, the company invested heavily in New Mexico to offer massive floor space dedicated to its embedded multi-die interconnect bridge technology. This provides a cost-effective alternative to the standard methods used by rivals. Operating domestic facilities also allows the company to easily win lucrative defense contracts that strictly require secure North American supply chains, a hurdle its Asian competitors simply cannot clear.

Story Continues

Finally, to capture more market share, Intel is aggressively rolling out its next-generation three-dimensional stacking technologies known as Foveros Omni and Foveros Direct. These advanced offerings enable direct copper-to-copper bonding, which drastically reduces power consumption while multiplying bandwidth between individual chiplets. The company is also pioneering the industry transition toward glass substrates, representing a massive material science upgrade over traditional organic bases that permits significantly larger chip packages. By expanding massive facilities in Malaysia and scaling its hybrid bonding techniques to achieve sub-ten-micron bump pitches, the company is equipping cloud hyperscalers with the necessary engineering tools required to build custom AI accelerators that can genuinely rival traditional monolithic processors.

Financials Not up There but Stabilizing

Intel has faced significant challenges over the past decade, recording negative compound annual growth rates of -0.46% for revenue and -45.59% for earnings. While INTC stock has risen approximately 61% during this period, the majority of that appreciation occurred after the U.S. government acquired roughly a 10% stake in the company.

There are modest signs of stabilization in the financial performance. Over the past nine quarters, Intel has beaten consensus earnings estimates on six occasions. The most recent quarter also delivered beats on both revenue and earnings.

Revenue declined 4% year-over-year (YoY) to $13.7 billion. The client computing segment, which remains the largest, fell 7% to $8.2 billion. However, this segment could see improvement in the coming quarters with the launch of the companyвҖҷs first AI PC platform built on the Intel 18A process, the Intel Core Ultra Series 3. Revenue from the data center and AI segment grew 9% to $4.7 billion, while the Foundry business increased 4% to $4.5 billion.

Earnings per share rose 15% to $0.15, exceeding the $0.08 consensus estimate. This marked the second consecutive quarter of beating earnings expectations.

Cash flow from operations for 2025 totaled $9.7 billion, up from $8.3 billion in the prior year. The company ended the quarter with $14.3 billion in cash and equivalents, comfortably exceeding its short-term debt of $2.5 billion.

Valuation metrics are mixed. The forward P/E ratio of 104.21 is well above the sector median of 21.63, but the forward P/S of 4.70 and forward P/CF of 17.72 appear more reasonable relative to sector medians of 2.97 and 16.50, respectively.

Analyst Opinion on INTC Stock

Considering all, analysts still rate INTC stock a consensus вҖңHold.вҖқ The mean target price has already been surpassed, whereas the high target price of $66 denotes an upside potential of about 30% from current levels. Out of 45 analysts covering the stock, five have a вҖңStrong BuyвҖқ rating, one has a вҖңModerate BuyвҖқ rating, 34 have a вҖңHoldвҖқ rating, one has a вҖңModerate SellвҖқ rating, and four have a вҖңStrong SellвҖқ rating.

news.Title: Cisco Systems (CSCO) Recently Broke Out Above the 50-Day Moving Average
news.Content:
From a technical perspective, Cisco Systems (CSCO) is looking like an interesting pick, as it just reached a key level of support. CSCO recently overtook the 50-day moving average, and this suggests a short-term bullish trend.

The 50-day simple moving average is a widely used technical indicator that helps determine support or resistance levels for different types of securities. It's one of three major moving averages, but takes precedent because it's the first sign of an up or down trend.

Over the past four weeks, CSCO has gained 5.5%. The company is currently ranked a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), another strong indication the stock could move even higher.

The bullish case only gets stronger once investors take into account CSCO's positive earnings estimate revisions. There have been 6 higher compared to none lower for the current fiscal year, and the consensus estimate has moved up as well.

With a winning combination of earnings estimate revisions and hitting a key technical level, investors should keep their eye on CSCO for more gains in the near future.

news.Title: вҖҳPeak MemoryвҖҷ Panic Is вҖҳOverblownвҖҷ Says Mizuho: Buy the Sandisk Stock Dip
news.Content:
Mizuho Securities (MFG) recently announced that the dip in memory stocks offers a вҖңBuyвҖқ opportunity, especially for Micron (MU) and SanDisk Corporation (SNDK). But this isnвҖҷt just another вҖҳbuy the dipвҖҷ call. The firm explains the bull thesis in a way that every investor, especially those scared of volatility and buying at peak, needs to listen to.

After calling the fears about declining memory usage in AI infrastructure вҖҳoverblownвҖҷ, analysts at Mizuho explained that TurboQuantвҖҷs success is a bull thesis, not a bear thesis, as many are calling it out to be. Vijay Rakesh, in a note to clients, pointed out that improvements in technology often result in an increase in demand rather than a decrease. For instance, virtual machines were expected to reduce server demand, but had the opposite impact. DeepSeekвҖҷs low-cost AI training gave rise to fears that companies in the U.S. might be overspending. While that was true, the low cost only resulted in an accelerated AI growth, resulting in even higher spending, especially from the likes of Microsoft (MSFT), OpenAI, and Meta Platforms (META). Similarly, the use of fiber optics instead of copper may offer 10 times the bandwidth, but this could again result in higher spending rather than lower.

More News from Barchart

NikeвҖҷs Real Problem IsnвҖҷt Sales: ItвҖҷs Relevance

Why Are ORCL Stock Investors So Excited About Oracle Layoffs?

Dear Costco Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for May 1

Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines.

These are valid points and considering how early we are in AI development, it is unlikely that memory demand will go down just because algorithms have changed the ways in which data is processed. It only means we can process more informatin at a time, allowing us to progress faster. There is no reason to slow down progress and save on memory costs when AI is quite literally a winner takes all race for the most part.

About SanDisk Stock

Headquartered in Milpitas, California, SanDisk is engaged in providing high-performance memory and storage solutions, offering solid state drives for gaming consoles, PCs, and desktops. Its end clients include businesses, IT organizations, individual consumers, mobile devices, and embedded systems markets as well as device manufacturers.

The release of TurboQuant algorithm caused a significant decline in SNDK stockвҖҷs value but it has recovered well so far this month returning 13.3%. The volatility in the stock continues to provide an attractive entry point to investors who believe the memory demand isnвҖҷt going away anytime soon.

Story Continues

SanDiskвҖҷs earnings growth has been staggering, yet it only trades at a forward price-to-earnings of 18.07 times, well below the S&P 500 IndexвҖҷs forward price-to-earnings of just above 21 times. Looking at the consensus earnings growth expectations of 118% in 2027 and negative in 2028, one can understand the pessimism. The market believes the memory demand will go down. However, we know that this also means companies will be reluctant to increase capacity when the market expects a decline in demand. If the supply constraints are likely to stay, all investors need is the Mizuho bull thesis to play out, resulting in a вҖңBuy and HoldвҖқ strategy ideal at current levels.

SanDisk Delivers Strong Earnings

For the second quarter of 2026, revenue came in at $3,025 billion, which surpassed the companyвҖҷs own projections. This showed a 31% growth from the previous quarter and 61% rise year-over-year (YOY). Segment-wise, Edge revenue was $1,678 million, consumer sales $907 million, and data center revenue clocked in at $440 million, with a progressive increase of 64% from the previous quarter. Non-GAAP gross margin surged to 51.1% as compared to the previous quarterвҖҷs 29.9%. Non-GAAP earnings per share increased to $6.20 from $1.22, as compared to the previous quarter.

The company reduced its debt by $750 million, and closed the quarter with $1,539 million in cash and $603 million in debt. This strengthening of the balance sheet is vital for a company operating in a sector known for its extreme cyclical nature.

What Are Analysts Saying About SanDisk Stock

After the recent recovery in stock price, SanDisk stock is closing in on its mean target price of $752.24, as per estimates from 20 different analysts. This suggests the valuation discount is narrowing and the market may well be waking up to the scenario Mizuho and other analysts have pointed to. In the last two weeks, three different analysts have assigned price targets of $875, $900, and $1000, which suggests the sentiment may be turning bullish again.

news.Title: еӨ–иіҮ3жңҲзӢӮз ҚеҸ°иӮЎйҖҫ8,300е„„  жҸҗж¬ҫиҝ‘еҚғе„„еүөеҸІдёҠеҗҢжңҹз¬¬еӣӣеӨ§
news.Content:
еҸ—зҫҺдјҠй–ӢжҲ°еҪұйҹҝпјҢеӨ–иіҮ3жңҲеӨ§з ҚеҸ°иӮЎгҖҒд№ҹжҸҗж¬ҫиҝ‘еҚғе„„е…ғпјҒиӯүжңҹеұҖеүҜеұҖй•·й»ғд»ІиұӘ7ж—ҘжҢҮеҮәпјҢ2026е№ҙ3жңҲе–®жңҲеӨ–йҷёиіҮиіЈи¶…дёҠеёӮж«ғиӮЎзҘЁж–°еҸ°е№Ј8,306.08е„„е…ғпјҢеүө2022е№ҙд»ҘдҫҶе–®жңҲиҲҮеҗҢжңҹз¬¬1еӨ§иіЈи¶…пјҢиҖҢ3жңҲе–®жңҲеӨ–иіҮж·ЁеҢҜеҮә31е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖҒзҙ„ж–°еҸ°е№Ј977е„„е…ғпјҢеүөеҸІдёҠеҗҢжңҹз¬¬4еӨ§ж·ЁеҢҜеҮәзҙҖйҢ„гҖӮ

дёҚйҒҺпјҢй»ғд»ІиұӘеј·иӘҝпјҢз”ұж–јд»Ҡе№ҙ1гҖҒ2жңҲеӨ–иіҮеӨ§жөҒе…ҘпјҢеӣ жӯӨд»Ҡе№ҙеүҚдёүжңҲзҙҜиЁҲж·ЁеҢҜе…Ҙ177.91е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖҒзҙ„ж–°еҸ°е№Ј5,690е„„е…ғпјҢд»ҚеүөдёӢеҸІдёҠеҗҢжңҹз¬¬дёҖеӨ§зҙҜиЁҲж·ЁеҢҜе…ҘзҙҖйҢ„гҖӮзӣ®еүҚеӨ–иіҮеӯҳйҮҸд»Қжңү3,378.77е„„зҫҺе…ғзҡ„ж°ҙдҪҚгҖӮ

й»ғд»ІиұӘжҢҮеҮәпјҢжҲӘиҮід»Ҡе№ҙ3жңҲеә•пјҢеӨ–иіҮеҠ йҷёиіҮ3жңҲиіЈи¶…еҸ°иӮЎйҒ”ж–°еҸ°е№Ј8,306е„„е…ғпјҢдёҚеғ…еүөе–®жңҲз¬¬дёҖеӨ§иіЈи¶…пјҢд№ҹжҳҜеҗҢжңҹз¬¬дёҖеӨ§иіЈи¶…пјҢиҖҢд»Ҡе№ҙйҰ–еӯЈеӨ–йҷёиіҮиіЈи¶…йҮ‘йЎҚйҒ”6,751.89е„„е…ғпјҢдәҰжҳҜеҸІдёҠз¬¬дёҖеӨ§иіЈи¶…гҖӮ

иӯүжңҹеұҖзөұиЁҲпјҢеӨ–иіҮ3жңҲе–®жңҲж·ЁеҢҜеҮә31е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖҒзҙ„ж–°еҸ°е№Јзҙ„977е„„е…ғпјҢеҜ«дёӢеҗҢжңҹз¬¬еӣӣеӨ§зҙҖйҢ„пјҢеҸІдёҠ3жңҲж·ЁеҢҜеҮәжңҖеӨ§йҮҸеҮәзҸҫеңЁ2020е№ҙзҡ„104е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖҒе…¶ж¬ЎжҳҜ2022е№ҙзҡ„69е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖҒ2025е№ҙзҡ„58е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖӮ

й»ғд»ІиұӘжҢҮеҮәпјҢе„ҳз®Ў3жңҲеӨ–иіҮж·ЁеҢҜеҮәиҝ‘еҚғе„„е…ғпјҢдҪҶд»Ҡе№ҙз¬¬дёҖеӯЈеӨ–йҷёиіҮж·ЁеҢҜе…Ҙ178.52е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢжҠҳеҗҲж–°еҸ°е№Јзҙ„5,709е„„е…ғпјҢд»ҚеҜ«дёӢ2022е№ҙд»ҘдҫҶпјҢеҗҢжңҹз¬¬дёҖеӨ§ж·ЁеҢҜе…ҘгҖӮ

зӮәдҪ•еӨ–иіҮ3жңҲеӨ§иіЈи¶…еҸ°иӮЎпјҢдҪҶж·ЁеҢҜеҮәйҮ‘йЎҚдёҰжІ’жңүеҗҢжӯҘеӨ§йҮҸпјҢй»ғд»ІиұӘиӘҚзӮәпјҢеҺҹеӣ иӨҮйӣңпјҢеӣ зӮәеҸ°иӮЎ3жңҲжіўеӢ•еӨ§пјҢеӣ жӯӨеӨ–иіҮеӨ§иҲүиіЈи¶…жҳҜеӣ жҮүжҲ°дәӢжғ…жіҒжіўеӢ•жүҖиҮҙгҖӮиҮіж–јж·ЁеҢҜеҮәгҖҒж·ЁеҢҜе…ҘйҷӨдәҶиӮЎеёӮеӣ зҙ еӨ–пјҢи·ҹеӨ–иіҮж“ҚдҪңзӯ–з•Ҙд№ҹжңүй—ңгҖӮ

й»ғд»ІиұӘиЎЁзӨәпјҢзөұиЁҲзҫҺд»ҘдјҠжҲ°дәӢиҮӘ2жңҲ28ж—Ҙй–ӢжҲ°д»ҘдҫҶпјҢеҸ°иӮЎйӣҶдёӯеёӮе ҙи·Ңе№…йҒ”6.17пј…пјҢеҗҢжңҹй–“йҹ“еңӢи·Ң12.48пј…гҖҒж—Ҙжң¬и·Ң9.31пј…гҖҒдёҠжө·и·Ң6.72пј…гҖҒйҰҷжёҜи·Ң5.69пј…гҖҒзҫҺеңӢйҒ“з“Ҡи·Ң4.71пј…гҖҒзҫҺеңӢйӮЈж–ҜйҒ”е…Ӣи·Ң2.96пј…гҖҒзҫҺеңӢиІ»еҚҠи·Ң2.25пј…гҖҒж–°еҠ еқЎи·Ң0.65пј…пјҢеҸ°иӮЎиЎЁзҸҫд»Ӣж–јеңӢйҡӣдё»иҰҒиӮЎеёӮд№Ӣй–“гҖӮ

news.Title: Big Tech stocks suddenly look cheap
news.Content: It could be time to code a few tech stock buys into your portfolio. "The underperformance of the technology sector is also starting to generate attractive valuation opportunities for investors as its valuation, relative to expected consensus growth, has fallen below that of the global aggregate market," Goldman Sachs strategist Peter Oppenheimer wrote in a new note on Tuesday. Oppenheimer continued, "These factors ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Goldman Sachs notes that the technology sector's underperformance has created attractive valuation opportunities.
вҖў Tech valuations relative to expected consensus growth have fallen below the global aggregate market.
вҖў Hyperscalers like Microsoft and Amazon face concerns about the potential returns on their massive AI capital expenditures.
вҖў Investors fear that AI investment costs are impacting cash flows and balance sheets.
вҖў The Magnificent Seven group of stocks has collectively lost $1.1 trillion in market cap as of early April 2026.
news.Keywords: tech sector valuation, AI capital expenditures, hyperscalers, cash flow concerns, market cap loss, investment opportunity

news.Title: Microsoft Unveils $10B Japan AI Investment, Expands Asia Cloud Push
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Microsoft ( NASDAQ:MSFT ) is expanding its AI infrastructure footprint in Asia with a newly announced $10 ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Microsoft is investing $10 billion in Japan over the next four years to scale cloud capacity and advance AI capabilities.
вҖў This investment reinforces Microsoft's earlier $2.9 billion commitment made in April 2024.
вҖў The company plans to train one million AI engineers by 2029 and expand cybersecurity partnerships.
вҖў Microsoft is collaborating with Sakura Internet and SoftBank's telecom unit for GPUs and computing resources.
вҖў This move follows similar investments in Singapore ($5.5 billion) and Thailand ($1 billion), indicating a coordinated Asian expansion.
news.Keywords: Japan AI investment, cloud capacity, AI engineers, cybersecurity partnerships, Asian expansion, infrastructure

news.Title: 3 Growth Stocks Worth $3,000 of Your Money -- Even in This Market
news.Content: Market volatility is up, and stock prices are down. However, that doesn't mean it's time to panic. Instead, now is the time to scour the market looking for great buys. While value stocks have been outperforming so far in 2026, that has rarely been the case over the past two decades. As such, investors should be looking for solid growth stocks at attractive prices in the market. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue В» ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Amazon (AMZN) is considered an underappreciated growth stock, with sales growing 86% to $716.9 billion and operating income up 3.5x to $80 billion over five years.
вҖў The stock trades at an attractive forward P/E of 27 times this year's estimates, a discount to peers like Walmart and Costco.
вҖў Amazon is growing its retail business faster, seeing strong operating leverage due to AI and automation investments.
вҖў Its cloud computing unit (AWS) is also experiencing fast growth.
вҖў The company's attractive valuation and multiple growth levers make it a great stock to buy at current levels.
news.Keywords: e-commerce, cloud computing, AI investments, automation, stock valuation, operating leverage, growth levers

news.Title: Intel to Buy Back $14.2 Billion Fab 34 Stake From Apollo
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Intel ( NASDAQ:INTC ) is moving to reassert control over a critical manufacturing asset, agreeing to acquire Apollo Global Management's ( NYSE:APO ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Intel is acquiring Apollo Global Management's 49% stake in the Fab 34 joint venture for $14.2 billion.
вҖў Fab 34 produces semiconductors using Intel 4 and Intel 3 process technologies, central to Intel's advanced node manufacturing.
вҖў The transaction is funded by cash on hand and approximately $6.5 billion in new debt issuances.
вҖў Intel expects the transaction to contribute to earnings per share and support its credit profile starting in 2027.
вҖў This move suggests Intel is placing increased strategic value on full ownership of key manufacturing capacity.
news.Keywords: Fab 34, joint venture, manufacturing asset, process technology, capital structure, earnings per share

news.Title: Palantir Could Be First Mover in Defense AI -- Oracle, Microsoft to Follow
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Wedbush analyst Dan Ives expects Palantir ( NASDAQ:PLTR ), Oracle ( NYSE:ORCL ) and Microsoft ( ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Palantir (PLTR), Oracle (ORCL), and Microsoft (MSFT) are expected to lead AI integration in defense operations.
вҖў Defense AI is viewed as a long-term spending trend, with Palantir as a first mover and Oracle/Microsoft capturing infrastructure and enterprise layers.
вҖў Palantir's U.S. government revenue reached $570 million in Q4 2025, up 66% year-over-year.
вҖў Oracle's cloud infrastructure revenue climbed 84% year-over-year to $4.9 billion.
вҖў Microsoft's Azure business expanded 39% year-over-year, and it holds a roughly 27% stake in OpenAI.
news.Keywords: defense AI, AI integration, government revenue, cloud infrastructure, Azure, OpenAI stake

news.Title: Why April 10 Could Be a Big Day for the Stock Market
news.Content: The market has been anything but quiet. Earlier this year, stocks struggled to overcome mounting concerns about artificial intelligence (AI) and private credit, among other signs of frothiness. Then the Iran war triggered a real sell-off, sending the major indexes briefly into correction territory. Since then, the market has bobbed and weaved, largely on headline-driven news about when the conflict could end. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue В» Another potential catalyst for ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The market is sensitive to the Iran war's impact on inflation, particularly the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
вҖў Crude oil prices topped $100, with economists expecting the March Consumer Price Index (CPI) to surge.
вҖў The Cleveland Fed's Inflation Nowcasting tool projects CPI to rise by 0.84% from February and 3.25% year-over-year.
вҖў Strong March jobs data (178,000 jobs added, unemployment at 4.3%) eases recession concerns but makes Fed rate cuts less likely.
вҖў Investors should not trade on CPI reports but watch for deviations from expectations.
news.Keywords: Iran war, inflation, CPI, oil prices, Federal Reserve, jobs report, interest rates

news.Title: Bank of America resets Apple stock forecast
news.Content: Apple ( AAPL ) stock has lost about 4.6% year to date, at the time of writing, Monday afternoon, April 6, according to Yahoo Finance . Meanwhile, the SPDR S&P 500 index ( SPY ) is down close to 3.5% in the same period. And while Apple is slightly lagging the S&P 500, so are the rest of the Magnificent 7 members. The rest of the Magnificent 7 stocks are also down in the same period: Microsoft ( MSFT ) is down almost 23%. Tesla ( TSLA ) is down 21.8%. Meta ( ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Apple's stock is down 4.6% year-to-date, slightly lagging the S&P 500 and other Magnificent 7 members.
вҖў Apple's AI strategy involves betting AI will become a commodity, keeping capital expenditures low ($14 billion vs. hyperscalers' $700 billion).
вҖў The new MacBook Neo, priced at $599, sold out quickly and could unlock a $32 billion TAM in 2026.
вҖў Bank of America estimates the MacBook Neo could add $0.03 to Apple's EPS if it captures 10% market share.
вҖў The Neo model is expected to drive adoption among first-time Mac buyers, potentially increasing services use.
news.Keywords: Apple stock, AI strategy, capital expenditure, MacBook Neo, TAM, EPS, market share

news.Title: Apple's iPhone sales continue to surge as iPhone 17 outpaces iPhone 16
news.Content: AppleвҖҷs ( AAPL ) iPhone continued strong sales last month, thanks to the popularity of the iPhone 17 line, Bernstein analysts wrote in an investor note on Monday. According to the report, Global iPhone sell-through вҖ” that is, sales of iPhones from retailers to customers вҖ” increased 26% year over year in February. Overall, the analysts say iPhone 17 unit sales are up 20% versus last yearвҖҷs ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Apple's iPhone sales increased 26% year-over-year in February, driven by the iPhone 17 line.
вҖў iPhone 17 unit sales are up 20% versus last year's iPhone 16 through September and January.
вҖў Average Selling Price (ASP) climbed 6.9% year-over-year in February, improving each month since the iPhone 17 launch.
вҖў China provided the biggest boost to ASP, with 20.2% year-over-year growth.
вҖў The new MacBook Neo could provide an additional $0.03 earnings per share for 2026 if it captures 10% market share in the entry-level laptop space.
news.Keywords: iPhone sales, iPhone 17, ASP, MacBook Neo, EPS, market share, memory supply crunch

news.Title: Microsoft Stock Is Down 30% From Its Peak. History Says This Is What Happens Next.
news.Content: Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) has been one of the worst big tech stocks to own this year. It's trading down over 23% in 2025 alone, and down around 31% from its all-time highs. While that may cause some investors to panic, I don't think it should. I think Microsoft will likely ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Microsoft (MSFT) shares are down over 23% in 2025 and 31% from all-time highs, presenting a potential buying opportunity.
вҖў The company's business model, with subscription-based products and cloud computing, provides continual cash flows.
вҖў Over the past decade, Microsoft's stock has experienced significant pullbacks, notably in late 2022-early 2023.
вҖў AI spending fears are cited as a reason for the current sell-off, but Microsoft is profiting from its cloud infrastructure supporting AI models.
вҖў Microsoft shares are trading near their cheapest P/E valuation in a decade, suggesting a potential rebound.
news.Keywords: stock performance, buying opportunity, subscription revenue, cloud computing, AI spending, valuation, P/E ratio

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎ3жңҲйңҮзӣӘж“ӢдёҚдҪҸжҠ•иіҮзҶұпјҒ0050жҡҙеўһ42иҗ¬жҠ•иіҮдәә еүөеҸ°иӮЎETFж–°зҙҖйҢ„
news.Content:
еҸ°иӮЎжӯ·з¶“еӢ•зӣӘзҡ„ 3 жңҲпјҢдҪҶ ETF жҠ•иіҮзҶұеәҰжңӘиҰӢйҷҚжә«пјҢж“ҡйӣҶдҝқзөҗз®—жүҖзөұиЁҲиҮі 4/2 з•¶е‘ЁпјҢжңҖеј·жҢҮжЁҷе…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈ 50(0050-TW) е–®жңҲжҡҙеўһйҖҫ 42 иҗ¬дәәпјҢзёҪжҠ•иіҮдәәж•ёйҒ” 274.5 иҗ¬дәәпјҢз©©еұ…дәәж°ЈзҺӢеҜ¶еә§пјҢдёҰж”№еҜ«еҸ°иӮЎ ETF е…Ёж–°зҙҖйҢ„гҖӮ

зөұиЁҲ 3 жңҲ ETF жҠ•иіҮдәәж•ёеўһеҠ жҺ’иЎҢпјҢ0050 д»Ҙе–®жңҲж–°еўһ 42.1 иҗ¬дәәж”№еҜ«жӯ·е№ҙдҫҶе–®жңҲж–°й«ҳзҙҖйҢ„пјҢд»Ҡе№ҙз•°и»ҚзӘҒиө·зҡ„еҮұеҹәеҸ°зҒЈ TOP50(009816-TW) е–®жңҲд№ҹжҝҖеўһ 33.8 иҗ¬еҗҚжҠ•иіҮдәәеұ…ж¬ЎпјҢдё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•· (00981A-TW)гҖҒзҫӨзӣҠеҸ°зҒЈзІҫйҒёй«ҳжҒҜ (00919-TW) еҲҶеҲҘеўһеҠ  15 иҗ¬гҖҒ14 иҗ¬дәәпјҢеҜҢйӮҰз§‘жҠҖ (0052-TW) жңҲеўһ 7.5 иҗ¬дәәпјҢиҖҢз”«еҲҶеүІе®ҢжҲҗжҒўеҫ©дәӨжҳ“зҡ„е…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈ 50 жӯЈ 2(00631L-TW) дәҰеўһеҠ  6.7 иҗ¬дәәгҖӮ

жі•дәәеҲҶжһҗпјҢйӣ–зҫҺдјҠжҲ°дәӢйҖ жҲҗйңҮзӣӘпјҢдҪҶ AI йңҖжұӮдҝқжҢҒеј·еӢҒпјҢеҸ°иӮЎдјҒжҘӯеҒҡзӮәдҫӣжҮүйҸҲдёӯеҝғпјҢй«ҳеәҰеҸ—жғ е…Ёзҗғз”ўжҘӯжһ¶ж§ӢпјҢеңЁеҮәеҸЈеј·еӢҒдёӢпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬еңӢзҷјжңғгҖҒдё»иЁҲзёҪиҷ•йҖЈзәҢдёҠдҝ®еҸ°зҒЈ 2026 GDP жҲҗй•·зҺҮй җжңҹгҖӮжңӘдҫҶйҡЁи‘—жҲ°дәӢи¶Ёз·©пјҢиіҮйҮ‘йҮҚеӣһи»ҢйҒ“еҫҢпјҢдҫқйҒҺеҫҖжӯ·еҸІз¶“й©—е°ҮжҢҒзәҢз”ұйҫҚй ӯж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎе„Әе…ҲеҸ—жғ гҖӮ

0050 ж“ҒжңүдёҚйҢҜйҒҺеҸ°иӮЎжјІеӢўд№Ӣзү№иүІпјҢдё”еңЁиіҮйҮ‘йқ’зқһж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎдёӢпјҢиҮӘ AI иө·жӯҘжҲҗй•·зҡ„ 2023 е№ҙд»ҘдҫҶиҮі 2026/3/20пјҢиҮәзҒЈ 50 жҢҮж•ёзҙҜз©Қеҗ«жҒҜзёҪе ұй…¬ 208.8%пјҢеӢқеҮәеӨ§зӣӨеҗ«жҒҜе ұй…¬зҺҮ 160.7% зҙ„ 48 еҖӢзҷҫеҲҶй»һгҖӮ

йҖҷиӮЎй•·жңҹжҠ•иіҮ 0050 зҡ„еҠӣйҒ“пјҢд№ҹжҢҒзәҢе№ҙиј•еҢ–гҖӮи§ҖеҜҹ 3 жңҲд»Ҫ 0-19 жӯІж–°еўһй–ӢжҲ¶ 4.7 иҗ¬пјҢ20-30 жӯІж–°еўһй–ӢжҲ¶ 4.9 иҗ¬пјҢеқҮијғ 2 жңҲзҙ„еҖҚж•ёжҲҗй•·пјӣеңӢе…§жңҖеӨ§иӯүеҲёжҘӯиҖ…е…ғеӨ§иӯүеҲёд№ҹе…¬еёғпјҢ2025 е№ҙ 12 жӯІд»ҘдёӢе…’з«Ҙй–ӢжҲ¶е№ҙеўһзҺҮзӘҒз ҙ 3 жҲҗпјҢ0050гҖҢз©©еұ…е…’з«Ҙ / зҲёеӘҪжңҖж„ӣеӯҳиӮЎжЁҷзҡ„гҖҚгҖӮ

еҸҰеӨ–пјҢ00631L ж–јиҝ‘жңҹе®ҢжҲҗеҲҶеүІеҫҢпјҢ1 ејөеғ№ж јзҙ„ 20 е…ғдёҠдёӢжҢҒзәҢеҗёеј•е°ҸиіҮж№§йҖІпјҢеҸҜжңӣеҶҚзӮә ETF жҺҖиө·жӣҙй«ҳзҡ„дәӨжҳ“зҶұеәҰгҖӮ

news.Title: йҖЈеҒҮеҫҢеҸ°иӮЎеӨ§жјІ ж–°еҸ°е№ЈжІ’и·ҹйҖІгҖҒйҷ·31.9е…ғеғ№дҪҚзӣӨж•ҙ
news.Content:
жё…жҳҺйҖЈеҒҮйҒҺеҫҢпјҢеҸ°иӮЎйҡЁеңӢйҡӣиӮЎеёӮеҸҚеҪҲеё¶еӢ•пјҢдёҠжј”иЈңжјІиЎҢжғ…пјҢдҪҶзҫҺдјҠиЎқзӘҒеҶҚеәҰеҚҮжә«пјҢе·қжҷ®й–Ӣе—ҶжҜҖж»…жҖ§жү“ж“ҠеҖ’ж•ёпјҢе°ҺиҮҙеңӢйҡӣжІ№еғ№зәҢжҸҡгҖҒзҫҺе…ғжҢҮж•ёеҸҲиЎқдёҠ100еӨ§й—ңпјҢе„ҳз®ЎеҸ°иӮЎеӨ§жјІдҪңж”¶пјҢдҪҶеӣ еӨҡз©әеӣ зҙ еӨҫйӣңпјҢж–°еҸ°е№Је…ҢзҫҺе…ғиө°еӢўеңЁеҚҖй–“йңҮзӣӘпјҢеҢҜйҠҖеқҰиЁҖпјҢз•¶еүҚж¶ҲжҒҜйқўзӣёз•¶еҸҚиҰҶпјҢеҫҲйӣЈй җжңҹеҢҜзҺҮиө°еҗ‘пјҢж•ҙй«”еёӮе ҙж“ҚдҪңд№ҹзӣёе°Қи§ҖжңӣпјҢй җиЁҲе…ҲеңЁ31.9е…ғеғ№дҪҚдёҠдёӢзӣӨж•ҙзӮәдё»гҖӮ

зҫҺиӮЎеүҚдёҖдәӨжҳ“ж—Ҙе…Ёйқўж”¶жјІпјҢеё¶еӢ•еҸ°иӮЎ7ж—Ҙй–Ӣй«ҳиө°й«ҳпјҢеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёзөӮе ҙеӨ§жјІ657.39й»һпјҢдҪҶеҸ°еҢ—йҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙиӮЎеҢҜдёҰжңӘеҗҢжӯҘиө°жҸҡпјҢж–°еҸ°е№ЈзӣӨдёӯеҚҮиІ¶дә’иҰӢпјҢдёҰжңӘи·ҹйҡЁеҸ°иӮЎдёҠжј”еҸҚж”»иЎҢжғ…пјҢзөӮе ҙж”¶еңЁ31.967е…ғгҖҒе°ҸеҚҮ0.5еҲҶпјҢеёӮе ҙзӣёе°Қи§ҖжңӣдёӢпјҢзёҪжҲҗдәӨйҮҸзё®ж°ҙиҮі15.29е„„зҫҺе…ғгҖӮ

7ж—Ҙдё»иҰҒдәһе№ЈеӨ§иҮҙиө°жҸҡпјҢйҹ“е…ғеӨ§жјІ0.81пј…жңҖеј·гҖҒе…¶ж¬ЎжҳҜдәәж°‘е№ЈжҸҡеҚҮ0.37пј…гҖҒжҳҹе№ЈжјІ0.11пј…гҖҒж–°еҸ°е№ЈеҚҮ0.02пј…пјҢеғ…ж—Ҙең“еӣһиІ¶0.19пј…гҖӮ

е°Қж–јж•ҙй«”еёӮе ҙиө°еӢўпјҢеӨ®иЎҢеӨ–еҢҜеұҖй•·и”Ўзғұж°‘еүҮиӘҚзӮәпјҢзӣ®еүҚдёҚеӨӘиғҪз”Ёеҹәжң¬йқўдҫҶзңӢеёӮе ҙпјҢж•ҙй«”еұҖеӢўйӮ„жҳҜеҸ–жұәж–јжІ№еғ№еҸҠжҲ°зҲӯеҪұйҹҝпјҢ3жңҲжІ№еғ№йЈҶз ҙ100зҫҺе…ғеӨ§й—ңпјҢеҸ°иӮЎеҸҚжҮүйҮҚжҢ«пјҢдҪҶзӣ®еүҚжІ№еғ№йӮ„еңЁ100зҫҺе…ғд»ҘдёҠпјҢдҪҶеёӮе ҙдҝЎеҝғе·Іеӣһз©©пјҢж•ҙй«”ж°Јж°ӣиҪүи¶ЁжЁӮи§ҖпјҢзҫҺиӮЎиІ»еҚҠеӣһеҲ°3жңҲеҲқй«ҳй»һпјҢеҸ°иӮЎиЎЁзҸҫеҗҢжӯҘеӣһз©©пјҢеҫҢзәҢйҷӨйқһжІ№еғ№иҝ‘дёҖжӯҘеҫҖдёҠйЈҶпјҢеҗҰеүҮеёӮе ҙеҸҚжҮүе·Ізӣёе°Қи¶Ёз·©гҖӮ

еҢҜйҠҖдё»з®ЎжҢҮеҮәпјҢдё»иҰҒжҳҜйҮҸиғҪжёӣз·©пјҢгҖҢзӣёијғдёҠе‘ЁеҸҠдёҠдёҠе‘ЁиЎЁзҸҫпјҢд»ҠеӨ©ж•ҙй«”зўәеҜҰжё…ж·ЎдёҖй»һгҖҚпјҢеӨ–иіҮж•ҙй«”жҳҜйӣҷеҗ‘ж“ҚдҪңпјҢиҖҢеҮәеҸЈе•ҶйӮ„еңЁзӯү32е…ғеғ№дҪҚпјҢж°ӣеңҚдёҠеҸҲиҪүзӮәжғңе”®пјҢдҪҶеңЁ31.99е…ғдёҠж–№жҷӮд»ҚжңүйҖІе ҙжӢӢеҢҜпјҢжҲҗзӮәеҢҜеғ№ж”Ҝж’җеҠӣйҒ“пјҢе…Ёж—ҘдёҰжңӘиІ¶з ҙ32е…ғж•ҙж•ёгҖӮ

еҢҜйҠҖдё»з®Ўд№ҹиӘӘпјҢеңЁдёӢеҚҲ3й»һеҚҠеҫҢд»ҚжңүйғЁеҲҶйҖІеҸЈе•ҶйҖІе ҙпјҢдё»иҰҒжҳҜеёӮе ҙеҺҹжң¬иӘҚзӮәж–°еҸ°е№ЈжңғеңЁйҖЈеҒҮеҫҢиЈңжјІпјҢйӣ–жңүй җжңҹеҝғзҗҶжІ’зӯүеҲ°зҗҶжғіеғ№дҪҚпјҢеҸӘеҘҪеңЁе°ҫзӣӨйҖІе ҙиІ·еҢҜпјҢеӨ®иЎҢд№ҹз©ҚжҘөйҖІе ҙжҸҗдҫӣжөҒеӢ•жҖ§пјҢеј·еӢўе°ҮеҢҜеғ№е®ҲеңЁ32е…ғдёӢж–№гҖӮ

еҢҜйҠҖдё»з®ЎеҲҶжһҗпјҢзҫҺдјҠжҲ°зҲӯзӣёз•¶еҸҚиҰҶпјҢеҺҹжң¬еӮіеҮәиҚ·е§ҶиҢІжө·еіҪжңүж©ҹжңғеұҖйғЁи§Је°ҒпјҢдҪҶеҫҢзәҢдјҠжң—еҶҚеәҰжӢ’зө•зҫҺеңӢжҸҗеҮәзҡ„еҒңзҒ«жңҖеҫҢйҖҡзү’пјҢж•ҙй«”иҖҢиЁҖйҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙеҸӘиғҪгҖҢдё”иө°дё”зңӢгҖҚпјҢиӮЎеҢҜеҸҚжҮүйҲҚеҢ–пјҢеӨ§йғЁеҲҶжІ’жңүзү№е®ҡж–№еҗ‘пјҢйӮ„жҳҜеҒҸи§ҖжңӣзӮәдё»пјҢзҹӯз·ҡжҢҒзәҢеңЁ31.9е…ғдёҠдёӢеҫҳеҫҠгҖӮ

еҢҜйҠҖдё»з®Ўд№ҹиӘӘпјҢж¶ҲжҒҜйқўеё¶еӢ•иіҮйҮ‘йқўпјҢдҪҶж•ҙй«”ж¶ҲжҒҜж··дәӮпјҢгҖҢдёӯеҚҲе·қжҷ®е—ҶиҒІгҖҒжҷҡдёҠеҸҲж”№и®ҠиӘӘжі•гҖҚпјҢжңғи®“еҜҰиіӘйңҖжұӮиІ·иіЈи¶Ёз·©пјҢж•ҙй«”ж°Јж°ӣзӣёз•¶и§ҖжңӣпјҢеӣ жӯӨжӯӨжҷӮеҚҖй–“йңҮзӣӘж јеұҖзҹӯжңҹйӣЈд»ҘжүӯиҪүгҖӮ

news.Title: Morning Brief: A sharp warning from Goldman Sachs
news.Content: рҹ‘Ӣ Good morning! Markets spent much of Monday in a state of confused optimism over Iran, sending markets slightly into the green, though oil prices rose. The S&P 500 ( ^GSPC ) gained 0.4%, the Dow ( ^DJI ) 0.3%, and the Nasdaq ( ^IXIC ) 0.5%. With threats, deadlines, and by-the-minute news changes, Iran and the Strait of Hormuz continue to drive the day. news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Markets showed confused optimism over Iran, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.4%, Dow 0.3%, and Nasdaq 0.5%.
вҖў Oil prices rose, and the Iran war continues to drive market sentiment.

news.Title: иӢұзү№зҲҫеј·ж”»е…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқ еӮізҲӯеҸ–и°·жӯҢ.дәһйҰ¬йҒңиЁӮе–®
news.Content:
[ йқһеҮЎж–°иҒһ ]иЁҳиҖ…й»ғйқ–жЈ»,ж”қеҪұи©№жҳҺжЁә

иӢұзү№зҲҫиІЎеӢҷй•·жҢҮеҮәпјҢе°ҒиЈқжҘӯеӢҷе·ІжҲҗзӮәзӣ®еүҚд»Је·ҘйғЁй–ҖгҖҢжңҖе…·жҪӣеҠӣгҖҚзҡ„дёҖз’°пјҢжңҖж–°еӮіеҮәиӢұзү№зҲҫжӯЈиҲҮGoogleеҸҠAmazonжҙҪи«Үе…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқеҗҲдҪңпјҢиҰҸжЁЎеҸҜиғҪйҒ”жҜҸе№ҙж•ёеҚҒе„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢдёҚйҒҺпјҢиӢұзү№зҲҫд»Је·ҘйғЁй–Җ2025е№ҙд»Қиҷ•иҷ§жҗҚзӢҖж…ӢпјҢдё”еңЁе…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқеёӮе ҙпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»д»ҘCoWoSжҠҖиЎ“д»ҚеҚ дё»е°Һең°дҪҚгҖӮ

Intel Newsroomе®ҳж–№еҪұзүҮпјҡгҖҢиӢұзү№зҲҫжӯЈеңЁзӘҒз ҙзү©зҗҶз•Ңйҷҗзҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҖ§пјҢжҺЎз”ЁFoverosе°ҒиЈқжҠҖиЎ“пјҢе’ҢEMIBеөҢе…ҘејҸеӨҡжҷ¶зүҮдә’йҖЈпјҢжҲ‘еҖ‘е°Үе°ҒиЈқе°әеҜёж“ҙеӨ§еҲ°зӣ®еүҚжҘӯз•ҢжЁҷжә–зҡ„8еҖҚпјҢеҲ°2028е№ҙе°Үи¶…йҒҺ12еҖҚгҖӮгҖҚ

иӢұзү№зҲҫе…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқжңҚеӢҷпјҢиҝ‘жңҹиҝҺдҫҶйҮҚеӨ§зӘҒз ҙпјҢеӮіеҮәжӯЈеңЁи·ҹGoogleе’ҢдәһйҰ¬йҒңпјҢжҙҪи«Үе…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқеҗҲдҪңиЁӮе–®пјҢд»ЈиЎЁTPUе’ҢTrainiumжҷ¶зүҮпјҢйғҪеҸҜиғҪжҺЎз”ЁиӢұзү№зҲҫзҡ„EMIBжҠҖиЎ“пјҢеҗҲдҪңжЎҲиҰҸжЁЎеҸҜйҒ”еҲ°жҜҸе№ҙж•ёеҚҒе„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢеёӮе ҙжҢҮеҮәпјҢIntelжңүжңӣжҶ‘и—үе°ҒиЈқжҠҖиЎ“еҲҮе…Ҙй«ҳжҜӣеҲ©еёӮе ҙпјҢжҲҗзӮәе…¶жҷ¶ең“д»Је·ҘжҘӯеӢҷзҝ»иә«зҡ„йҮҚиҰҒй—ңйҚөгҖӮ

еҸ°з¶“йҷўз”ўз¶“иіҮж–ҷеә«зёҪзӣЈ еҠүдҪ©зңҹпјҡгҖҢзӣёијғж–јеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жүҖдҪҝз”Ёзҡ„еӨ§йқўз©ҚзҹҪдёӯд»ӢеұӨпјҢиӢұзү№зҲҫеҸӘйңҖиҰҒеңЁйҖЈжҺҘиҷ•пјҢеөҢе…ҘжҜ”ијғзҙ°е°Ҹзҡ„зҹҪж©ӢпјҢе°ұиғҪеӨ жңүж•ҲжҸҗеҚҮе®ғзҡ„йқҲжҙ»жҖ§пјҢд»ҘеҸҠеӨ§е№…йҷҚдҪҺз”ҹз”ўжҲҗжң¬гҖӮгҖҚ

е°ҚжӯӨGoogleе’ҢдәһйҰ¬йҒңжӢ’зө•зҪ®и©•пјҢдҪҶиӢұзү№зҲҫеңЁж–°еўЁиҘҝе“ҘжҙІе’ҢйҰ¬дҫҶиҘҝдәһпјҢйғҪжңүеёғе»әйҫҗеӨ§зҡ„е…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқз”ўиғҪпјҢеҗҢжҷӮйҮқе°ҚзҺ»з’ғеҹәжқҝзҡ„жҠҖиЎ“з ”зҷјд№ҹзӣёијғй ҳе…ҲпјҢиў«иҰ–зӮәжҳҜ2026е№ҙд№ӢеҫҢпјҢи§Јжұәи¶…еӨ§е°әеҜёжҷ¶зүҮж•ЈзҶұе’ҢеӮіијёз“¶й ёзҡ„й—ңйҚөжҠҖиЎ“гҖӮдёҚйҒҺпјҢиӢұзү№зҲҫиҰҒжҢ‘жҲ°еҸ°з©Қйӣ»CoWoS е…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқең°дҪҚпјҢжҲҗзӮәжҪӣеңЁзҡ„з«¶зҲӯе°ҚжүӢпјҢе°ұжңүиӢұзү№зҲҫеүҚе“Ўе·ҘжҢҒдҝқз•ҷж…ӢеәҰпјҢиӘҚзӮәе®ўжҲ¶жҲ–иЁұжңғжңүеҸҰдёҖеұӨж“”жҶӮпјҢи§ҖжңӣиӢұзү№зҲҫиғҪеҗҰеҰӮжңҹе®ҢжҲҗж“ҙе»әгҖӮ

еҸ°з¶“йҷўз”ўз¶“иіҮж–ҷеә«зёҪзӣЈ еҠүдҪ©зңҹпјҡгҖҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еӣ зӮәй•·жңҹд»ҘдҫҶпјҢжҳҜдёҚи·ҹе®ўжҲ¶дҫҶйҖІиЎҢз«¶зҲӯзӮәж ёеҝғзҡ„дёҖеҖӢе•ҶжҘӯжЁЎејҸпјҢжүҖд»ҘдёҚеӨӘеҸҜиғҪеӣ зӮәе®ўжҲ¶е°ӢжұӮз¬¬дәҢдҫӣжҮүдҫҶжәҗпјҢиҖҢжғЎж„ҸеҺ»зё®жёӣд»–зҡ„жҷ¶ең“з”ўиғҪпјҢеӣ зӮәйҖҷеҖӢе°ҮжңғжҗҚеҸҠд»–дҪңзӮәдёӯз«Ӣд»Је·ҘйҫҚй ӯзҡ„дҝЎиӯҪгҖӮгҖҚ

иӢұзү№зҲҫд»ҚйқўиҮЁжҢ‘жҲ°пјҢд»Је·ҘйғЁй–Җ2025е№ҙйӮ„иҷ•иҷ§жҗҚзӢҖж…ӢпјҢиҖҢеңЁе…ҲйҖІе°ҒиЈқеёӮе ҙпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жҠҖиЎ“дҫқз„¶еҚ дё»е°Һең°дҪҚгҖӮпјҲиЁҳиҖ…й»ғйқ–жЈ»гҖҒи©№жҳҺжЁәпјҸеҸ°еҢ—жҺЎиЁӘе ұе°Һпјү

и§ҖзңӢеҺҹж–Ү...

ж‘әз–ҠiPhoneеӮіеңЁеҜҢеЈ«еә·и©Ұз”ў! й җиЁҲдёӢеҚҠе№ҙе•Ҹдё–

еңӢйҡӣйӢҒеғ№зӢӮйЈҶ4е№ҙж–°й«ҳ йӣ»еӯҗд»Је·Ҙе» жҜӣеҲ©жӢүиӯҰе ұ

иҝҺжҺҘеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жі•иӘӘиЎҢжғ… жңүжңӣеӮ¬еӢ•еҸ°иӮЎеҸҚеҪҲж”»еӢў

news.Title: дёӯжқұжҲ°зҒ«гҖҢе°Ҳе®¶е–ҠжңүйҢўеҲҘж”ҫзҫҺеңӢгҖҚпјҒжҸӯеҸ°з©Қйӣ»гҖҢзөӮжҘөзҺ©жі•гҖҚпјҡжңӘдҫҶйғҪжҳҜз¬‘зҡ„дәә
news.Content:
иІЎз¶“дёӯеҝғпјҸз¶ңеҗҲе ұе°Һ

йҡЁи‘—дёӯжқұеұҖеӢўеҚҮжә«пјҢе…ЁзҗғиіҮжң¬еёӮе ҙжіўеӢ•еҠ еҠҮпјҢдёҚе°‘жҠ•иіҮдәәйҮҚж–°жҖқиҖғиіҮз”ўй…ҚзҪ®ж–№еҗ‘гҖӮжңҖж–°еӘ’й«”еҲҶжһҗжҢҮеҮәпјҢеңЁAIз”ўжҘӯжҢҒзәҢж“ҙејөд№ӢйҡӣпјҢзңҹжӯЈжҺҢжҸЎй—ңйҚөе„ӘеӢўзҡ„дјҒжҘӯжңӘеҝ…дҫҶиҮӘзҫҺеңӢпјҢиҖҢжҳҜиў«иҰ–зӮәгҖҢAIи»ҚеӮҷз«¶иіҪжңҖеӨ§еҸ—зӣҠиҖ…гҖҚзҡ„еҸ°з©Қйӣ»пјҢиў«еҪўе®№зӮәдёҚи«–е“ӘдёҖе®¶з§‘жҠҖе·Ёй ӯеӢқеҮәпјҢйғҪиғҪз©©е®ҡзҚІеҲ©зҡ„гҖҢзөӮжҘөзҺ©е®¶гҖҚгҖӮ

еҸ°з©Қйӣ»2025е№ҙзҮҹж”¶йҒ”1224е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢе№ҙжҲҗй•·зҝ»еҖҚпјҢе…¶дёӯAIзӣёй—ңж”¶е…ҘеӨ§е№…жҸҗеҚҮгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸж°‘иҰ–иіҮж–ҷз…§пјү

ж №ж“ҡгҖҠThe Motley FoolгҖӢеёӮе ҙз ”з©¶пјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»2025е№ҙзҮҹж”¶еӨ§е№…жҲҗй•·пјҢе…Ёе№ҙйҠ·е”®йҒ”1224е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢе№ҙеўһе№…йҒ”100%пјҢе…¶дёӯAIзӣёй—ңзҮҹж”¶жӣҙеӨ§е№…жҲҗй•·48%пјҢйЎҜзӨәз”ҹжҲҗејҸAIиҲҮй«ҳж•ҲиғҪйҒӢз®—йңҖжұӮпјҢжӯЈеҝ«йҖҹжҺЁеҚҮе…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢжҷ¶зүҮйңҖжұӮгҖӮеҲҶжһҗжҢҮеҮәпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬ NVIDIAгҖҒMicrosoftгҖҒAmazon зӯүз§‘жҠҖе·Ёй ӯжҢҒзәҢж“ҙеӨ§AIиіҮжң¬ж”ҜеҮәпјҢй–“жҺҘеё¶еӢ•жҷ¶ең“д»Је·ҘйңҖжұӮжҸҗеҚҮгҖӮе ұе‘ҠжҢҮеҮәпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»зӣ®еүҚжҺҢжҸЎзҙ„70%зҡ„е…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢеёӮе ҙпјҢеҚ ж“ҡз”ўжҘӯй—ңйҚөең°дҪҚпјҢзӣёијғз«¶зҲӯе°ҚжүӢIntelиҲҮSamsungпјҢеңЁиЈҪзЁӢжҠҖиЎ“иҲҮиүҜзҺҮж–№йқўд»Қе…·й ҳе…Ҳе„ӘеӢўпјҢеҪўжҲҗйӣЈд»Ҙж’јеӢ•зҡ„иӯ·еҹҺжІігҖӮ

еёӮе ҙй җдј°AIжҺЁи«–еёӮе ҙиҰҸжЁЎе°Үз”ұ1060е„„зҫҺе…ғжҲҗй•·иҮі2030е№ҙзҡ„2550е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢжңӘдҫҶеҗ„йЎһAIжҮүз”Ёзҷјеұ•йғҪйңҖд»°иіҙе…ҲйҖІжҷ¶зүҮж”ҜжҸҙгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸж°‘иҰ–иіҮж–ҷз…§пјү

еёӮе ҙд№ҹзңӢеҘҪAIжҺЁи«–пјҲAI inferenceпјүеёӮе ҙиҰҸжЁЎе°Үеҫһзӣ®еүҚ1060е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢжҲҗй•·иҮі2030е№ҙзҡ„2550е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢжңӘдҫҶз„Ўи«–AIжҮүз”ЁиҗҪең°ж–јйӣІз«ҜгҖҒйҶ«зҷӮжҲ–иҮӘеӢ•еҢ–й ҳеҹҹпјҢйғҪйңҖиҰҒжӣҙеӨ§йҮҸе…ҲйҖІжҷ¶зүҮж”Ҝж’җпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еҸҜжңӣжҢҒзәҢеҸ—жғ AIй•·жңҹжҲҗй•·и¶ЁеӢўгҖӮеҲҶжһҗжҢҮеҮәпјҢиҲҮе…¶жҠјжіЁе–®дёҖAIи»ҹй«”жҲ–е№іеҸ°пјҢдёҚе°‘жҠ•иіҮдәәй–Ӣе§ӢиҪүеҗ‘гҖҢиіЈйҸҹеӯҗзҡ„дәәгҖҚпјҢд№ҹе°ұжҳҜжҸҗдҫӣйҒӢз®—еҹәзӨҺзҡ„жҷ¶зүҮиЈҪйҖ е•ҶгҖӮз”ұж–јAIзҷјеұ•д»Қиҷ•ж—©жңҹйҡҺж®өпјҢзӣёй—ңдјҒжҘӯиіҮжң¬ж”ҜеҮәй җиЁҲжҢҒзәҢж”ҖеҚҮпјҢи®“еҸ°з©Қйӣ»еңЁе…Ёзҗғз§‘жҠҖдҫӣжҮүйҸҲдёӯз¶ӯжҢҒй—ңйҚөи§’иүІгҖӮйҡЁи‘—ең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»йўЁйҡӘиҲҮз§‘жҠҖз«¶зҲӯеҚҮжә«пјҢеёӮе ҙиіҮйҮ‘еёғеұҖйҖҗжјёиҪүеҗ‘е…·еӮҷжҠҖиЎ“й–ҖжӘ»иҲҮз”ўжҘӯеЈҹж–·е„ӘеӢўзҡ„дјҒжҘӯпјҢе°Ҳе®¶иӘҚзӮәAIжҷӮд»Јзҡ„й•·жңҹиҙҸе®¶пјҢеҸҜиғҪдёҚжҳҜжңҖжңғеҜ«жј”з®—жі•зҡ„е…¬еҸёпјҢиҖҢжҳҜиғҪз©©е®ҡжҸҗдҫӣз®—еҠӣж ёеҝғзҡ„еҚҠе°Һй«”иЈҪйҖ йҫҚй ӯгҖӮ

гҖҠж°‘иҰ–ж–°иҒһз¶ІгҖӢжҸҗйҶ’жӮЁпјҡе…§е®№еғ…дҫӣеҸғиҖғпјҢжҠ•иіҮдәәж–јжұәзӯ–жҷӮжҮүеҜ©ж…Һи©•дј°йўЁйҡӘпјҢдёҰе°ұжҠ•иіҮзөҗжһңиҮӘиЎҢиІ иІ¬гҖӮ

news.Title: AIжӢӢе”®жҪ®пјҒеӨ–еӘ’й»һеҗҚгҖҢйҖҷ2жӘ”еҝ…й ҲиІ·йҖІгҖҚеҸ°з©Қйӣ»д№ҹдёҠжҰң
news.Content: иІЎз¶“дёӯеҝғпјҸе‘Ёеӯҹжјўе ұе°Һ е„ҳз®Ўиҝ‘жңҹз§‘жҠҖиӮЎйҒӯйҒҮжӢӢе”®пјҢе°ҺиҮҙжЁҷжҷ®500жҢҮж•ёд»Ҡе№ҙд»ҘдҫҶдёӢи·Ңзҙ„4пј…пјҢдҪҶеёӮе ҙе°Ҳе®¶жҷ®йҒҚиӘҚзӮәпјҢйҖҷжіўз”ұдәәе·Ҙжҷәж…§пјҲAIпјүй©…еӢ•зҡ„зүӣеёӮгҖҢйӮ„жІ’и·‘е®ҢгҖҚгҖӮж‘©ж №еЈ«дё№еҲ©пјҲMorganStanleyпјүй җиЁҲпјҢе…ЁзҗғAIзӣёй—ңж”ҜеҮәеңЁ2028е№ҙе°ҮйЈҶз ҙ3е…ҶзҫҺе…ғеӨ§й—ңпјҢдё”зӣ®еүҚйӮ„жңүй«ҳйҒ”80пј…зҡ„й җз®—е°ҡжңӘжҠ•е…ҘгҖӮеңЁиіҮйҮ‘зӢӮжҪ®еҫҢеӢҒеҚҒи¶ізҡ„иғҢжҷҜдёӢпјҢиҝ‘жңҹз§‘жҠҖиӮЎзҡ„еӣһжӘ”пјҢеҸҚиҖҢзӮәжҠ•иіҮдәәжӢүеҮәдәҶгҖҢдёҠи»ҠгҖҚзҡ„з©әй–“гҖӮе°ҚжӯӨпјҢе°ұжңүеӨ–еӘ’зү№еҲҘй»һеҗҚдәҶгҖҢйҖҷ2жӘ”еҖӢиӮЎгҖҚпјҢиӘҚзӮәжҳҜзӣ®еүҚеҚҒеҲҶеҖјеҫ—жҠ•иіҮдәәй—ңжіЁзҡ„жҪӣеҠӣжЁҷзҡ„гҖӮ ж‘©ж №еЈ«дё№еҲ©пјҲMorganStanleyпјүй җиЁҲпјҢе…ЁзҗғAIзӣёй—ңж”ҜеҮәеңЁ2028е№ҙе°ҮйЈҶз ҙ3е…ҶзҫҺе…ғеӨ§й—ңгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸеҸ°з©Қйӣ»пјү ж №ж“ҡжҠ•иіҮеӘ’й«” гҖҠTheMotleyFoolгҖӢ е ұе°ҺпјҢйӣ–з„¶жЁҷжҷ®500жҢҮж•ёиҝ‘жңҹеҸ—з§‘жҠҖиӮЎжӢӢе”®еҪұйҹҝиЎЁзҸҫз–Іи»ҹпјҢдҪҶйҖҷеҸҚиҖҢжҸӯзӨәдәҶAIжөӘжҪ®дёӢзҡ„гҖҢе„ӘеҠЈеҲҶж°ҙе¶әгҖҚгҖӮе ұе°ҺжҢҮеҮәпјҢй ҳе…Ҳзҡ„з§‘жҠҖе…¬еҸёйңҖжұӮдҫқиҲҠз©©еҒҘпјҢдё”иҲҮеёӮе ҙй җжё¬й«ҳеәҰеҗ»еҗҲпјҢзӣ®еүҚжӯЈжҳҜеҜ©иҰ–е…·еӮҷеҜҰиіӘзҚІеҲ©иғҪеҠӣгҖҒдё”иӮЎеғ№еӣһжӘ”еҫҢзҡ„гҖҢй«ҳеӢ•иғҪеҖӢиӮЎгҖҚзө•дҪіжҷӮж©ҹпјҢд»ҘдёӢеүҮзӮәйҖҷжіўеӣһжӘ”дёӯпјҢжңҖиў«зңӢеҘҪзҡ„2жӘ”AIйҫҚй ӯпјҡ 1гҖҒи»ҹй«”е®ҡзҫ©жңӘдҫҶпјҡPalantir TechnologiesпјҲPLTRпјү Palantirйӣ–з„¶иҝ‘жңҹиӮЎеғ№еҫһй«ҳй»һдҝ®жӯЈ28пј…пјҢдҪҶе…¶еҹәжң¬йқўеҚ»е‘ҲзҸҫзҲҶзҷјејҸжҲҗй•·гҖӮPalantirзҡ„ж ёеҝғз«¶зҲӯеҠӣеңЁж–је…¶гҖҢдәәе·Ҙжҷәж…§е№іеҸ°пјҲAIPпјүгҖҚпјҢиғҪе°Үж·ұеҘ§зҡ„AIжЁЎеһӢзӣҙжҺҘиҪүеҢ–зӮәдјҒжҘӯзҡ„зңҒйҢўе·Ҙе…·иҲҮзҚІеҲ©еј•ж“ҺпјҢе№«еҠ©дјҒжҘӯе’Ңж”ҝеәңж©ҹж§ӢеҚіжҷӮйҖЈзөҗж•ёж“ҡгҖҒжұәзӯ–иҲҮзҮҹйҒӢпјҢеҺ»е№ҙе…¶зҮҹж”¶жӣҙжҳҜжҲҗй•·56пј…пјҢз¬¬еӣӣеӯЈжӣҙеӣ зҫҺеңӢе•ҶжҘӯйңҖжұӮеҷҙзҷјпјҢзҮҹж”¶еўһй•·зҺҮиЎқдёҠ70пј…гҖӮйҖҷзЁ®иҲҮдјҒжҘӯзҮҹйҒӢж·ұеәҰз¶‘з¶Ғзҡ„зү№жҖ§пјҢи®“Palantirж“ҒжңүдәҶжҘөй«ҳзҡ„е®ўжҲ¶й»Ҹи‘—еәҰпјҢйӣ–з„¶зӣ®еүҚдј°еҖјеҒҸй«ҳпјҢдҪҶиӢҘзҮҹж”¶иҲҮеҲ©жҪӨдҝқжҢҒеј·еӢҒпјҢжәўеғ№иЎЁзҸҫд»Қиў«еёӮе ҙжҺҘеҸ—гҖӮ 2.жҷ¶зүҮиЈҪйҖ йңёдё»пјҡеҸ°з©Қйӣ»(TSMC) иә«зӮәе…Ёзҗғе…ҲйҖІиЈҪзЁӢзҡ„е”ҜдёҖд»ЈеҗҚи©һпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»пјҲTSMпјүеңЁAIз”ўжҘӯдёӯе…·еӮҷгҖҢз„ЎеҸҜеҸ–д»ЈгҖҚзҡ„ең°дҪҚпјҢдёҚеғ…ж“ҒжңүиҳӢжһңпјҲAppleпјүзӯүи¶…зҙҡеӨ§е®ўжҲ¶пјҢе…¶54пј…зҡ„зҮҹжҘӯеҲ©зӣҠзҺҮжӣҙеұ•зҸҫдәҶгҖҢз„ЎеҸҜеҸ–д»ЈгҖҚзҡ„е®ҡеғ№ж¬ҠгҖӮеңЁеҗ„еӨ§йӣІз«ҜеӨ§е» зҳӢжҗ¶жҷ¶зүҮзҡ„иғҢжҷҜдёӢпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»е°ҚжңӘдҫҶйңҖжұӮзҡ„иғҪиҰӢеәҰжҘөй«ҳпјҢй җиЁҲAIзӣёй—ңзҮҹж”¶е№ҙжҲҗй•·е°Үи¶…йҒҺ50пј…гҖӮд»Ҙзӣ®еүҚзҙ„24еҖҚзҡ„жң¬зӣҠжҜ”дҫҶзңӢпјҢжҳҜеҸғиҲҮAIжөӘжҪ®жңҖз©©еҒҘдё”е…·еӮҷжҖ§еғ№жҜ”зҡ„йҒёж“Ү

news.Title: Why NuScale Power Stock Sank 15.6% in March
news.Content: After NuScale Power (NYSE: SMR) stock plunged 26.5% in February, investors had high hopes the nuclear energy stock would recover in March. Sadly for them, though, this didn't come to pass for two reasons. For one, NuScale Power reported disappointing fourth-quarter 2025 financial results in late February, ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў NuScale Power (SMR) stock dropped 15.6% in March after a 26.5% fall in February.
вҖў Disappointing Q4 2025 financial results showed revenue of $31.5 million, a 15% decrease from Q4 2024.
вҖў Several firms issued pessimistic outlooks and price target reductions for NuScale Power stock.
вҖў Canaccord slashed its price target to $25 from $60; Goldman Sachs dropped its target to $14 from $20.
вҖў Despite revenue decrease, the company awaits commencement of commercial operations, targeting 2030.
news.Keywords: NuScale Power, financial results, revenue decline, price target reductions, commercial operations, small modular reactors

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎжјІ657й»һз ҙи§Ј"жё…жҳҺи®ҠзӣӨ" еҸ°з©ҚжјІ50е…ғй ҳи»Қж”¶еҫ©жңҲз·ҡ
news.Content:
иІЎз¶“дёӯеҝғпјҸе”җи©©жҷҙгҖҒиіҙж–Үи»’ еҸ°еҢ—е ұе°Һ

йҒҺеҺ»еёӮе ҙзёҪж“”еҝғзҡ„гҖҢжё…жҳҺи®ҠзӣӨйӯ”е’’гҖҚпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙдёҚеғ…жІ’зҷјз”ҹпјҢе‘ЁдәҢеӨ§зӣӨеҸҚиҖҢдёҠжј”е ұеҫ©жҖ§еҸҚеҪҲпјҢзөӮе ҙзӢӮйЈҶ657й»һпјҢж”¶еңЁ33,229й»һпјҢйҖҷиӮЎеј·еӢҒзҡ„иЈңжјІиЎҢжғ…пјҢжҲҗеҠҹж”¶еҫ©дёүиҗ¬дёүеҚғй»һеӨ§й—ңпјҢжӣҙиҲҮеё¶й ӯиЎқзҡ„еҸ°з©Қйӣ»пјҢз«ҷз©©жңҲз·ҡд№ӢдёҠгҖӮ

зҫҺдјҠеҒңзҒ«еҚ”иӯ°пјҢжҝҖеӢөзҫҺиӮЎгҖҒдәһиӮЎеҸҚеҪҲпјҢд№ҹи®“еҸ°иӮЎзӢӮзүӣпјҢжҺҷи„«жё…жҳҺйҖЈеҒҮи®ҠзӣӨз·Ҡз®Қе’’пјҢеңЁ4/7иӮЎзҒҪе‘Ёе№ҙпјҢжҸҡзңүеҗҗж°ЈгҖӮ

ж°‘иҰ–иЁҳиҖ… е”җи©©жҷҙпјҡгҖҢжҸ®еҲҘдј‘еёӮеүҚи§Җжңӣйҷ°йңҫпјҢжӣҙжү“з ҙжё…жҳҺйҖЈеҒҮеҫҢи®ҠзӣӨйӯ”е’’пјҢ4/7еҸ°иӮЎиҝҺдҫҶиЈңжјІиЎҢжғ…пјҢж—©зӣӨи·із©әеӨ§жјІиҝ‘700й»һгҖӮгҖҚ

зӣӨдёӯжңҖй«ҳи§ёеҸҠ33,264й»һпјҢдёҖеәҰзҚІеҲ©дәҶзөҗиіЈеЈ“ж№§зҸҫпјҢжјІе№…ж”¶ж–ӮпјҢдҪҶзөӮе ҙд»ҚдёҠжјІ657й»һпјҢжҲҗеҠҹж”¶еҫ©жңҲз·ҡпјҢж”¶еңЁ33,229й»һпјҢеҸҜжғңжҲҗдәӨйҮҸз·ҠйҒ”еҲ°5,654е„„гҖӮ

зөұдёҖжҠ•йЎ§еҲҶжһҗеё« йҷіз«Ӣе§”пјҡгҖҢдё»иҰҒйӮ„жҳҜдёӯжқұзҡ„жҲ°дәӢе…¶еҜҰйӮ„жІ’жңүи§ЈжұәпјҢе·қжҷ®еҸҲжҢҒзәҢең°ж”ҫи©ұпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬иҰҒзӮёж©ӢжЁ‘е•ҠгҖҒзҷјйӣ»е» зӯүзӯүзҡ„иЁҖиӘһпјҢжүҖд»ҘеёӮе ҙйӮ„жҳҜж“”еҝғпјҢеҒңзҒ«еҚ”иӯ°д№ӢеүҚпјҢжҢҮж•ёй җдј°еңЁеӯЈз·ҡд»ҘеҸҠжңҲз·ҡд№Ӣй–“еҒҡдёҖеҖӢеҚҖй–“йңҮзӣӘгҖӮгҖҚ

4/7ж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎиЎЁзҸҫгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸж°‘иҰ–ж–°иҒһпјү

4/16жі•иӘӘжңғеүҚпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еҗ№иө·еҸҚж”»иҷҹи§’пјҢж”¶еҫ©жңҲз·ҡпјҢдёҠжјІ50е…ғпјҢж”¶еңЁ1,860е…ғпјҢеё¶й ҳиҒҜйӣ»гҖҒеҸ°йҒ”йӣ»гҖҒиҒҜзҷјз§‘е·ЁдәәжӯёдҪҚпјҢCPOе…үйҖҡиЁҠпјҢеҸ—жғ Touch Taiwanеұ•иҰҪй җзҶұпјҢдёҠи©®гҖҒиЁҠиҠҜ-KYгҖҒжіўиӢҘеЁҒпјҢе…Ёдә®зҮҲжјІеҒңпјҢи»ҢйҒ“иЎӣжҳҹпјҢе•ҹзўҒгҖҒжҳҮйҒ”з§‘д№ҹеӨ§еҷҙзҷјпјҢзӮәеӨ§зӣӨжіЁе…Ҙеј·еҝғйҮқгҖӮ

4/7CPOе…үйҖҡиЁҠгҖҒи»ҢйҒ“иЎӣжҳҹж—ҸзҫӨеӨҡиЎЁзҸҫдә®зңјгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸж°‘иҰ–ж–°иҒһпјү

зөұдёҖжңҹиІЁеҲҶжһҗеё«зӣ§жҳұиЎЎпјҡгҖҢеҸ°иӮЎеҰӮжһңеӣһеҲ°еӨҡй ӯзҡ„и©ұпјҢеӨ§е®¶еҸҜд»Ҙй—ңжіЁжңҖиҝ‘иіҮйҮ‘жҜ”ијғйқ’зқһзҡ„ж—ҸзҫӨпјҢеғҸжҳҜиЁҳжҶ¶й«”жҲ–жҳҜABFијүжқҝпјҢжҲ–иҖ…жҳҜйҖҷеҖӢйӣ»жәҗз®ЎзҗҶпјҢеҸ°йҒ”йӣ»гҖҒеҸ°е…үйӣ»йҖҷдёҖйЎһпјҢйӮЈеҰӮжһңиӘӘзҫҺеңӢи·ҹдјҠжң—йҖҷйӮҠжңүйҖІдёҖжӯҘзҡ„иЎқзӘҒеҚҮжә«зҡ„жғ…жіҒпјҢйӮЈжҲ‘еҖ‘зҡ„иіҮйҮ‘еҸҜиғҪеҸҲжңғеӣһеҲ°еғҸеЎ‘еҢ–йЎһиӮЎпјҢеҺ»еҒҡдёҖеҖӢйҒҝйҡӘгҖӮгҖҚ

ијүжқҝдёүйӣ„еј·еӢўиЎЁж…ӢпјҢеҚ—йӣ»жјІеҒңйҺ–жӯ»пјҢдёүжҳҹDRAMе ұеғ№жјІеғ№пјҢеҗҢжЁЈи®“ж—әе®ҸгҖҒеҚ—дәһз§‘зӯүиЁҳжҶ¶й«”пјҢиЎЁзҸҫдёҚдҝ—пјҢйӣ–иӘӘең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»з…ҷзЎқпјҢе°ҡжңӘж•ЈеҺ»пјҢдҪҶAIзјәж–ҷиҲҮ4жңҲжі•иӘӘжңғиЎҢжғ…зңӢдҝҸпјҢжҲҗзӮәеӮ¬еӢ•еҸ°иӮЎзҡ„еҠ©зҮғеҠ‘гҖӮ

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎжјІ657й»һз ҙи§Ј"жё…жҳҺи®ҠзӣӨ" еҸ°з©ҚжјІ50е…ғй ҳи»Қж”¶еҫ©жңҲз·ҡ
news.Content:
иІЎз¶“дёӯеҝғпјҸе”җи©©жҷҙгҖҒиіҙж–Үи»’ еҸ°еҢ—е ұе°Һ

йҒҺеҺ»еёӮе ҙзёҪж“”еҝғзҡ„гҖҢжё…жҳҺи®ҠзӣӨйӯ”е’’гҖҚпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙдёҚеғ…жІ’зҷјз”ҹпјҢе‘ЁдәҢеӨ§зӣӨеҸҚиҖҢдёҠжј”е ұеҫ©жҖ§еҸҚеҪҲпјҢзөӮе ҙзӢӮйЈҶ657й»һпјҢж”¶еңЁ33,229й»һпјҢйҖҷиӮЎеј·еӢҒзҡ„иЈңжјІиЎҢжғ…пјҢжҲҗеҠҹж”¶еҫ©дёүиҗ¬дёүеҚғй»һеӨ§й—ңпјҢжӣҙиҲҮеё¶й ӯиЎқзҡ„еҸ°з©Қйӣ»пјҢз«ҷз©©жңҲз·ҡд№ӢдёҠгҖӮ

зҫҺдјҠеҒңзҒ«еҚ”иӯ°пјҢжҝҖеӢөзҫҺиӮЎгҖҒдәһиӮЎеҸҚеҪҲпјҢд№ҹи®“еҸ°иӮЎзӢӮзүӣпјҢжҺҷи„«жё…жҳҺйҖЈеҒҮи®ҠзӣӨз·Ҡз®Қе’’пјҢеңЁ4/7иӮЎзҒҪе‘Ёе№ҙпјҢжҸҡзңүеҗҗж°ЈгҖӮ

ж°‘иҰ–иЁҳиҖ… е”җи©©жҷҙпјҡгҖҢжҸ®еҲҘдј‘еёӮеүҚи§Җжңӣйҷ°йңҫпјҢжӣҙжү“з ҙжё…жҳҺйҖЈеҒҮеҫҢи®ҠзӣӨйӯ”е’’пјҢ4/7еҸ°иӮЎиҝҺдҫҶиЈңжјІиЎҢжғ…пјҢж—©зӣӨи·із©әеӨ§жјІиҝ‘700й»һгҖӮгҖҚ

зӣӨдёӯжңҖй«ҳи§ёеҸҠ33,264й»һпјҢдёҖеәҰзҚІеҲ©дәҶзөҗиіЈеЈ“ж№§зҸҫпјҢжјІе№…ж”¶ж–ӮпјҢдҪҶзөӮе ҙд»ҚдёҠжјІ657й»һпјҢжҲҗеҠҹж”¶еҫ©жңҲз·ҡпјҢж”¶еңЁ33,229й»һпјҢеҸҜжғңжҲҗдәӨйҮҸз·ҠйҒ”еҲ°5,654е„„гҖӮ

зөұдёҖжҠ•йЎ§еҲҶжһҗеё« йҷіз«Ӣе§”пјҡгҖҢдё»иҰҒйӮ„жҳҜдёӯжқұзҡ„жҲ°дәӢе…¶еҜҰйӮ„жІ’жңүи§ЈжұәпјҢе·қжҷ®еҸҲжҢҒзәҢең°ж”ҫи©ұпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬иҰҒзӮёж©ӢжЁ‘е•ҠгҖҒзҷјйӣ»е» зӯүзӯүзҡ„иЁҖиӘһпјҢжүҖд»ҘеёӮе ҙйӮ„жҳҜж“”еҝғпјҢеҒңзҒ«еҚ”иӯ°д№ӢеүҚпјҢжҢҮж•ёй җдј°еңЁеӯЈз·ҡд»ҘеҸҠжңҲз·ҡд№Ӣй–“еҒҡдёҖеҖӢеҚҖй–“йңҮзӣӘгҖӮгҖҚ

4/7ж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎиЎЁзҸҫгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸж°‘иҰ–ж–°иҒһпјү

4/16жі•иӘӘжңғеүҚпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еҗ№иө·еҸҚж”»иҷҹи§’пјҢж”¶еҫ©жңҲз·ҡпјҢдёҠжјІ50е…ғпјҢж”¶еңЁ1,860е…ғпјҢеё¶й ҳиҒҜйӣ»гҖҒеҸ°йҒ”йӣ»гҖҒиҒҜзҷјз§‘е·ЁдәәжӯёдҪҚпјҢCPOе…үйҖҡиЁҠпјҢеҸ—жғ Touch Taiwanеұ•иҰҪй җзҶұпјҢдёҠи©®гҖҒиЁҠиҠҜ-KYгҖҒжіўиӢҘеЁҒпјҢе…Ёдә®зҮҲжјІеҒңпјҢи»ҢйҒ“иЎӣжҳҹпјҢе•ҹзўҒгҖҒжҳҮйҒ”з§‘д№ҹеӨ§еҷҙзҷјпјҢзӮәеӨ§зӣӨжіЁе…Ҙеј·еҝғйҮқгҖӮ

4/7CPOе…үйҖҡиЁҠгҖҒи»ҢйҒ“иЎӣжҳҹж—ҸзҫӨеӨҡиЎЁзҸҫдә®зңјгҖӮпјҲең–пјҸж°‘иҰ–ж–°иҒһпјү

зөұдёҖжңҹиІЁеҲҶжһҗеё«зӣ§жҳұиЎЎпјҡгҖҢеҸ°иӮЎеҰӮжһңеӣһеҲ°еӨҡй ӯзҡ„и©ұпјҢеӨ§е®¶еҸҜд»Ҙй—ңжіЁжңҖиҝ‘иіҮйҮ‘жҜ”ијғйқ’зқһзҡ„ж—ҸзҫӨпјҢеғҸжҳҜиЁҳжҶ¶й«”жҲ–жҳҜABFијүжқҝпјҢжҲ–иҖ…жҳҜйҖҷеҖӢйӣ»жәҗз®ЎзҗҶпјҢеҸ°йҒ”йӣ»гҖҒеҸ°е…үйӣ»йҖҷдёҖйЎһпјҢйӮЈеҰӮжһңиӘӘзҫҺеңӢи·ҹдјҠжң—йҖҷйӮҠжңүйҖІдёҖжӯҘзҡ„иЎқзӘҒеҚҮжә«зҡ„жғ…жіҒпјҢйӮЈжҲ‘еҖ‘зҡ„иіҮйҮ‘еҸҜиғҪеҸҲжңғеӣһеҲ°еғҸеЎ‘еҢ–йЎһиӮЎпјҢеҺ»еҒҡдёҖеҖӢйҒҝйҡӘгҖӮгҖҚ

ијүжқҝдёүйӣ„еј·еӢўиЎЁж…ӢпјҢеҚ—йӣ»жјІеҒңйҺ–жӯ»пјҢдёүжҳҹDRAMе ұеғ№жјІеғ№пјҢеҗҢжЁЈи®“ж—әе®ҸгҖҒеҚ—дәһз§‘зӯүиЁҳжҶ¶й«”пјҢиЎЁзҸҫдёҚдҝ—пјҢйӣ–иӘӘең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»з…ҷзЎқпјҢе°ҡжңӘж•ЈеҺ»пјҢдҪҶAIзјәж–ҷиҲҮ4жңҲжі•иӘӘжңғиЎҢжғ…зңӢдҝҸпјҢжҲҗзӮәеӮ¬еӢ•еҸ°иӮЎзҡ„еҠ©зҮғеҠ‘гҖӮ

news.Title: гҖҠйҷёиӮЎгҖӢж»¬жҢҮжјІ0.26%пјҢзөӮжӯўйҖЈдәҢи·ҢгҖҒж—ҘKзҝ»зҙ…
news.Content:
MoneyDJж–°иҒһ 2026-04-07 17:22:06 ж–°иҒһдёӯеҝғ зҷјдҪҲ

дёӯеңӢж»¬ж·ұе…©еёӮдёүеӨ§жҢҮж•ёд»Ҡ(7)ж—ҘйӣҶй«”й–Ӣй«ҳпјҢж—©зӣӨе…©еёӮиЎқй«ҳеӣһжӘ”пјҢж·ұиӯүжҲҗжҢҮгҖҒеүөжҘӯжқҝжҢҮеҚҲеүҚзӣёз№јиҪүи·ҢпјӣеҚҲеҫҢеёӮе ҙеӣһжҡ–пјҢжҲӘиҮіж”¶зӣӨпјҢдёүеӨ§жҢҮж•ёйӣҶй«”е°Ҹе№…дёҠжјІгҖӮзӣӨйқўдёҠпјҢдёӯжқұжҲ°дәӢе°ҺиҮҙиҚ·е§ҶиҢІжө·еіҪж°®иӮҘдҫӣжҮүеҸ—йҳ»пјҢеҢ–еӯёеҺҹж–ҷжқҝеЎҠиө°й«ҳпјҢжңүж©ҹзҹҪжқҝеЎҠиө°еј·пјҢзҹіжІ№еҠ е·ҘиІҝжҳ“гҖҒзЁҖеңҹж°ёзЈҒзӯүжјІе№…еұ…еүҚпјӣзӣёијғдёӢпјҢйўЁйӣ»ж•ҙж©ҹиӮЎиө°дҪҺпјҢйҠҖиЎҢгҖҒдҝқйҡӘиӮЎжҷ®и·ҢпјҢиҲӘз©әйҒӢијёгҖҒе°Ҹе®¶йӣ»зӯүжқҝеЎҠи·Ңе№…еұ…еүҚгҖӮж•ҙй«”дҫҶзңӢпјҢе…©еёӮеҖӢиӮЎе‘Ҳжҷ®жјІж…ӢеӢўпјҢдёҠжјІеҖӢиӮЎиҝ‘4,000жӘ”пјҢж»¬жҢҮзөӮе ҙж”¶жјІ0.26%пјҢзөӮжӯўйҖЈдәҢи·ҢгҖҒж—ҘKзҝ»зҙ…гҖӮ

дёҠиӯүжҢҮж•ёд»Ҡж”¶ж–ј3890.16й»һпјҢдёҠжјІ10.06й»һжҲ–0.26%пјӣж·ұиӯүжҲҗжҢҮж”¶ж–ј13400.41й»һпјҢдёҠжјІ47.51й»һжҲ–0.36%пјӣеүөжҘӯжқҝжҢҮж”¶ж–ј3160.82й»һпјҢдёҠжјІ11.22й»һжҲ–0.36%пјӣз§‘еүө50ж”¶ж–ј1274.01й»һпјҢдёҠжјІ17.80й»һжҲ–1.42%пјӣдёӯиӯүA500ж”¶ж–ј5510.74й»һпјҢдёҠжјІ8.86й»һжҲ–0.16%гҖӮ

жҲҗдәӨйҮҸж–№йқўпјҢж»¬еёӮд»ҠжҲҗдәӨ7,239е„„е…ғ(дәәж°‘е№Ј,дёӢеҗҢ)пјҢж·ұеёӮжҲҗдәӨ8,905е„„е…ғпјҢе…©еёӮжҲҗдәӨйЎҚ1е…Ҷ6,144е„„е…ғпјҢијғеүҚдёҖдәӨжҳ“ж—ҘйҮҸжёӣе°‘гҖӮ

еҫһзӣӨйқўдёҠдҫҶзңӢпјҢеҢ–еӯёеҺҹж–ҷгҖҒзҹіжІ№еҠ е·ҘиІҝжҳ“гҖҒдә’иҒҜз¶Ійӣ»е•ҶгҖҒз…ӨзӮӯзӯүжқҝеЎҠе’ҢжҰӮеҝөиӮЎжјІе№…еұ…еүҚпјӣдҝқйҡӘгҖҒйҠҖиЎҢгҖҒиІҙйҮ‘еұ¬гҖҒеүөж–°и—ҘзӯүжқҝеЎҠе’ҢжҰӮеҝөиӮЎи·Ңе№…еұ…еүҚгҖӮ

йҰҷжёҜиӮЎеёӮд»Ҡж—Ҙеӣ еҫ©жҙ»зҜҖеҒҮжңҹз№јзәҢдј‘еёӮпјҢе°Үж–јжҳҺ(4/8)ж—ҘжҒўеҫ©жӯЈеёёдәӨжҳ“гҖӮ

гҖҠйҷёиӮЎгҖӢж»¬жҢҮи·Ң0.74%гҖҒйҡӘе®Ҳ3900й»һпјӣжҒҶжҢҮж—ҘKйҖЈдёүй»‘

гҖҠйҷёиӮЎгҖӢж»¬жҢҮеӨұе®Ҳ3900й»һгҖҒи·Ң0.8%пјӣжҒҶжҢҮжјІ0.15%

иіҮж–ҷдҫҶжәҗ- MoneyDJзҗҶиІЎз¶І

news.Title: Cantor Fitzgerald Raises Price Target for KLA Corp. (KLAC)
news.Content: KLA Corp. (NASDAQ: KLAC ) is one of the 10 best AI chip stocks to buy according to hedge funds . On March 30, Cantor Fitzgerald increased the price ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Cantor Fitzgerald raised KLA Corp. (KLAC) price target to $2,000 and maintained an Overweight rating, citing a multi-year secular upturn in the Semi Equipment industry.
вҖў Jefferies reduced its price target to $1,700 but reaffirmed a Buy rating, expecting revenues to reach $26 billion with EPS of $84 by 2030.
