news.Title: Is Micron the New Nvidia?
news.Content: When OpenAI commercially released ChatGPT in late 2022, Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) was primarily seen as a graphics chip designer marketing its products toward computer gaming and secondarily as a chip provider for cryptocurrency mining operations. What most investors didn't realize was that the company's hardware was even more versatile than people had given it credit for. From 2023 to 2025, its chipsets became ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Micron Technology (MU) stock is up 27% year-to-date and nearly 309% over the last 12 months, driven by the memory and storage supercycle in AI.
вҖў Micron supplies DRAM and NAND flash memory, which are becoming AI's latest bottleneck, with HBM being a key component for AI chips.
вҖў Big tech hyperscalers are forecast to spend $700 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026, and Micron is sold out of its 2026 HBM inventory.
вҖў While Micron is a key pillar in AI infrastructure, its application is more specialized than Nvidia's GPUs, making it unlikely to be the "new Nvidia" but enjoying an "Nvidia moment."
news.Keywords: AI, memory, HBM, semiconductor, stock performance

news.Title: Should You Forget Sandisk and Buy These 2 AI Stocks While They're Cheap?
news.Content: Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) was the best-performing stock within the Russell 1000 Index in the first quarter, with its share price surging 194%. The company benefited from a shortage in the NAND (flash) memory market, which helped drive up prices and led to huge revenue and earnings growth. While the NAND market is ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Sandisk (SNDK) was the best-performing stock in the Russell 1000 in Q1, surging 194% due to NAND flash memory shortages.
вҖў While the NAND market benefits from AI infrastructure, it's historically cyclical, making Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) potentially better long-term AI stock picks.
вҖў Nvidia's GPUs and CUDA software platform create a strong ecosystem for AI workloads, with attractive valuations (forward P/E of 21x current FY estimates).
вҖў Broadcom leads in AI networking and custom AI chips (ASICs), with its valuation dropping to 17.5x next fiscal year's estimates due to explosive growth potential.
news.Keywords: AI, NAND flash memory, semiconductors, valuation, long-term investment

news.Title: Did Alphabet Just Say Checkmate to Micron?
news.Content: Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) just dropped a bombshell that is sending waves through the stock market, especially in the memory chip sector. Before Alphabet's announcement, the assumption was that memory capacity for AI computing chips would be in a supply constraint for several years, leading to soaring memory prices. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Alphabet's (GOOG/GOOGL) TurboQuant algorithm reduces AI memory needs by six times, potentially impacting memory chip stocks like Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK).
вҖў The algorithm's open-source nature allows competitors to integrate it, causing fear in memory chip stocks.
вҖў The Jevons Paradox suggests that increased efficiency could lead to higher demand for memory chips as AI becomes more accessible.
вҖў Micron's Q3 outlook projects revenue of $33.5 billion, with gross margin increasing by 660 basis points, indicating strong demand despite the TurboQuant news.
news.Keywords: AI, memory, TurboQuant, Jevons Paradox, semiconductor

news.Title: Arm (ARM) Enters the Chip Market. HereвҖҷs Why IвҖҷm Bullish
news.Content: Arm HoldingsвҖҷ (ARM) entry into the chip market marks a meaningful shift in its business model. The company has long been known as the quiet power behind modern computing, licensing the central processing unit (CPU) designs that sit inside smartphones, data center chips, autos, and more. The stock is already up around 36% year-to-date, yet I remain bullish on ARM as the company moves beyond collecting royalties on othersвҖҷ success. Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Arm Holdings (ARM) is launching its first in-house data center chip, the AGI CPU, targeting a $100 billion server CPU market by 2030.
вҖў This move allows Arm to capture more value by selling silicon rather than just licensing designs, with projections of $15 billion in chip revenue by fiscal 2031.
вҖў CPUs are becoming more important for AI orchestration and inference, and Arm's architecture excels where power efficiency is critical.
вҖў Key partners for the AGI CPU include Meta (META) and OpenAI, leveraging Arm's existing ecosystem strength.
news.Keywords: AI, CPU, data center, semiconductor, market expansion

news.Title: What Is the Jevons Paradox and What Does It Mean for Micron and Sandisk Investors After Google's Revolutionary AI Breakthrough?
news.Content: Last week, Alphabet 's (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) Google unveiled TurboQuant, an algorithm that marked a significant advancement in artificial intelligence (AI). Researchers said the algorithm reduces memory usage "by at least 6x and delivers up to 8x speedup, all with zero accuracy loss, redefining AI efficiency." This could reduce the amount of memory needed by as much as 83%. In the wake of this news, shares of memory chipmakers Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google unveiled TurboQuant, an AI algorithm reducing memory usage by up to 6x and increasing speed by 8x.
вҖў This breakthrough caused memory chip stocks like Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK) to fall amid fears of reduced demand.
вҖў The Jevons Paradox suggests that increased efficiency could lower costs and thus increase demand for memory chips, fueling AI adoption.
вҖў Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh reiterated buy ratings on Micron and Sandisk, citing TurboQuant as a positive catalyst for AI adoption and component spending.
news.Keywords: AI, memory, TurboQuant, Jevons Paradox, semiconductor

news.Content: NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA ) is among the stocks in focus as Jim Cramer highlighted a market yearning for the status quo ante . Cramer highlighted the companyвҖҷs recent deal ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Jim Cramer commented that NVIDIA's (NVDA) price-to-earnings (PE) multiple "makes no sense at all," comparing it to a "boring old bank stock with bad credits."
вҖў Cramer highlighted NVIDIA's $2 billion deal with Marvell Technology (MRVL) for an AI infrastructure plan, noting it's a smart move for NVIDIA to attract companies not currently using its platform.
вҖў NVIDIA rallied 5.6%, while Marvell shot up 13% following the announcement.
вҖў Cramer believes NVIDIA's software ecosystem is a significant asset beyond its chips.
news.Keywords: AI, semiconductors, valuation, PE multiple, partnership

news.Title: Bank of America resets Marvell stock price target after Nvidia deal
news.Content: Marvell Technology stock ( MRVL ) has got Wall Street talking. It is suddenly back on the radar. And interestingly, this time, Wall Street is turning more bullish. Shares of Marvell closed at $99.05 on March 31 after jumping 12.8% that single day, as per Yahoo Finance , extending an already strong run. In fact, Marvell is now up more than 60% over the past year, far beyond the broader S&P 500 . So whatвҖҷs behind the latest single-day bull run? A fresh price target hike from one of ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Bank of America raised its price target on Marvell Technology (MRVL) to $125 from $110, maintaining a Buy rating, following a partnership with Nvidia.
вҖў The partnership involves Marvell powering a new generation of AI infrastructure through Nvidia's NVLink Fusion ecosystem.
вҖў Marvell brings custom XPUs and networking switches, while Nvidia provides CPUs, networking chips, and AI interconnect technology.
вҖў Marvell reported record fiscal 2026 revenue of $8.195 billion (up 42% YoY), driven largely by AI demand, with data center revenue at $1.65 billion (74% of total).
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, semiconductors, Nvidia partnership, price target, revenue growth

news.Title: Marvell Gets NvidiaвҖҷs $2B вҖҳVote of ConfidenceвҖҷ: Analysts Strike a Bullish Chord
news.Content: Nvidia (NVDA) is investing $2 billion in Marvell Technology (MRVL) to boost its AI ecosystem through NVLink Fusion technology. This partnership links Marvell to NvidiaвҖҷs AI factories and AI-RAN networks for custom AI infrastructure. Marvell provides custom XPUs and compatible networking, while Nvidia ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia (NVDA) is investing $2 billion in Marvell Technology (MRVL) to enhance its AI ecosystem through NVLink Fusion technology.
вҖў Marvell will provide custom XPUs and networking, while Nvidia contributes CPUs, NICs, DPUs, and interconnects.
вҖў Analysts from Bank of America Securities and RBC Capital raised their price targets for MRVL, citing the strategic win and Marvell's leading role in connectivity and custom chips.
вҖў The partnership is expected to drive steady long-term sales for Marvell in custom chips and optics, and opens new markets via NVLink Fusion and telecom.
news.Keywords: AI ecosystem, semiconductors, NVLink Fusion, partnership, analyst ratings

news.Title: Top Stock Reports for Broadcom, Visa & Home Depot
news.Content: Thursday, April 2, 2026 The Zacks Research Daily presents the best research output of our analyst team. Today's Research Daily features new research reports on 16 major stocks, including Broadcom Inc. (AVGO), Visa Inc. (V) and The Home Depot, Inc. (HD), as well as a micro-cap stock BayFirst Financial Corp. (BAFN). The Zacks microcap research is unique as our research content on these small and under-the-radar companies is the only research of its type in the country. These research reports have been hand-picked from the roughly 70 reports published by our analyst team today. You can see all of today’s ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Broadcom (AVGO) is experiencing strong momentum driven by AI semiconductors and VMware integration, with AI revenues expected to surge 140% YoY to $10.7 billion in Q2 fiscal 2026.
вҖў Strong demand for its networking products and custom AI accelerators (XPUs) is a key driver, with AI networking revenues growing 60% YoY.
вҖў Visa (V) faces headwinds from rising client incentives and expenses, with analysts expecting adjusted costs to rise 11% in FY26.
вҖў Home Depot (HD) reported sales and EPS misses in Q4 fiscal 2025, with weak demand and margin pressure impacting results.

news.Title: Chip Stocks Fall After Trump Iran Warning
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . AI and chip stocks took a hit Thursday after President Donald Trump warned the U.S. could ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў AI and chip stocks experienced a hit after President Trump warned of potential US strikes against Iran, causing oil prices to spike.
вҖў Nvidia (NVDA) was down about 2%, AMD fell nearly 3%, and Broadcom (AVGO) and Qualcomm (QCOM) slid 2-3%.
вҖў Micron (MU) dropped around 5%, while Arm (ARM) and Marvell (MRVL) each fell about 4%.
вҖў Networking and optical names like Arista (ANET), Lumentum (LITE), and Coherent (COHR) also moved lower.
news.Keywords: Geopolitical risk, semiconductors, chip stocks, market sell-off, oil prices

news.Content: Cisco Systems вҖҷ CSCO gross margin has contracted sequentially over the trailing four quarters. In the second quarter of fiscal 2026, CSCO reported a non-GAAP gross margin of 67.5% compared with 68.1% in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, 68.4% in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 and 68.6% in the third ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Cisco Systems (CSCO) reported a sequential contraction in gross margin for four consecutive quarters, with Q2 fiscal 2026 non-GAAP gross margin at 67.5%.
вҖў The decline is attributed to negative impacts from product mix and higher memory costs, particularly for AI-related revenues.
вҖў Cisco expects Q3 fiscal 2026 gross margin to be 65.5-66.5%, indicating a further sequential decline.
вҖў The company expects AI orders exceeding $5 billion and over $3 billion in AI infrastructure revenues from hyperscalers in fiscal 2026.
news.Keywords: Gross margin, AI revenue, memory costs, product mix, Cisco

news.Title: US Lawmakers Propose Crackdown on Chip Tool Sales to China
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- US lawmakers unveiled legislation that seeks to crack down on exports of chipmaking tools to China, especially from allies including the Netherlands and Japan, in a bipartisan effort to help Washington further constrain BeijingвҖҷs technology ambitions. The draft bill, introduced in the House on Thursday, would strengthen existing restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment sales from firms like ASML Holding NV and Tokyo Electron Ltd. A companion version is expected to be released in the Senate later this month, according to people familiar with the matter. The goal is to subject these companies to the same, tougher curbs their ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў US lawmakers have introduced legislation (MATCH Act) to crack down on chipmaking tool exports to China, including from allies like the Netherlands and Japan.
вҖў The bill aims to strengthen existing restrictions on firms like ASML Holding NV and Tokyo Electron Ltd., subjecting them to tougher curbs faced by US rivals.
вҖў This includes banning engineers from maintaining and repairing tools at certain Chinese facilities, impacting Applied Materials Inc., Lam Research Corp., and KLA Corp.
вҖў The legislation seeks to align measures across US and foreign firms and broaden the scope of controlled technologies, potentially tightening restrictions for US firms as well.
news.Keywords: Export controls, China, semiconductors, chipmaking equipment, legislation

news.Content: Applied Materials AMAT, being the leading manufacturer of wafer fabrication equipment products, is benefiting from the huge demand from chip manufacturers serving the AI market and the supply shortage of memory chips in late 2025, as memory production is being redirected toward high-margin AI applications, including HBM and DDR5. ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Applied Materials (AMAT) is benefiting from high demand from chip manufacturers serving the AI market and a memory chip shortage.
вҖў The company expects its leading-edge foundry, logic, DRAM, and HBM businesses to be the fastest-growing WFE segments in 2026.
вҖў AMAT deepened its partnership with SK hynix and Micron Technology for R&D in next-generation memory for AI applications.
вҖў While competitors like Lam Research (LRCX) and ASML Holding (ASML) focus on specific areas, AMAT's broad product portfolio reduces reliance on single technology cycles.
news.Keywords: Semiconductor equipment, AI, memory chips, WFE, manufacturing

news.Title: AMAT, LRCX: Bank of America Is Still Pounding the Table on These 2 Chip Stocks
news.Content: Middle East tensions linked to the Iran war have pressured several semiconductor equipment stocks, pushing them sharply lower. The situation has reignited concern about global supply chain fragility, particularly access to helium and other materials essential for chip production. This is forcing the market to rethink which companies can hold their ground if disruptions linger or even escalate. Bank of America is leaning into that debate by keeping two semiconductor equipment giants at the top of its preference list, despite the latest drawdowns. This stance suggests ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Bank of America maintains a positive outlook on Applied Materials (AMAT) and Lam Research (LRCX) despite recent semiconductor stock drawdowns due to Middle East tensions.
вҖў AMAT is a $271.3B semiconductor equipment manufacturer with a forward P/E of 29.1x, collaborating with Micron (MU) on memory technology for AI.
вҖў LRCX, a $266.8B semiconductor equipment company, is collaborating with IBM on sub-1 nm logic scaling, trading at a forward P/E of 40.80x.
вҖў AMAT reported Q1 fiscal 2026 EPS of $2.39 (vs. $2.31 estimate) on revenue of $7.01B (up 3.1% YoY), with a "Strong Buy" rating and an average price target implying 12.6% upside.
news.Keywords: Semiconductor equipment, AI, stock valuation, analyst rating, collaboration

news.Title: Five Below and International Paper have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
news.Content: For Immediate Release Chicago, IL – April 2, 2026 – Zacks Equity Research shares Five Below FIVE as the Bull of the Day and International Paper IP as the Bear of the Day. In addition, Zacks Equity Research provides analysis on —Micron Technology, Inc. MU and Lam Research Corp. LRCX. Here is a synopsis of all three stocks: Bull of the Day: Let me tell you something, this is exactly the kind of stock that gets interesting when the market starts getting selective. This company has built a business model around trend-right merchandise, impulse purchases, and a price point that ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Five Below (FIVE) is highlighted as the Bull of the Day due to its business model focused on trend-right merchandise at accessible price points.
вҖў The company has a strong store growth runway and expanding margins, with current year EPS growth forecast at 17.54%.
вҖў International Paper (IP) is the Bear of the Day, facing cyclical demand weakness, restructuring costs, and uneven industrial activity.
вҖў Lam Research Corp. (LRCX) is mentioned as a company benefiting from memory chip growth, with expected earnings growth of 26.6% and 26.9% for the current and next year, respectively.
news.Keywords: Retail, discount, earnings growth, semiconductors, stock rating

news.Title: Can TSM Meet FY26 Margin Guidance Amid Fab Expansion Cost Pressures?
news.Content: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company TSM, also known as TSMC, has consistently delivered strong gross margins over the past few quarters despite mounting cost pressures from overseas fab expansion. To capitalize on the growing demand for advanced and artificial intelligence (AI) chips, the company is expanding production outside Taiwan, including new fabs in the United ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is expanding production globally, including five new fabrication facilities in Arizona, USA.
вҖў Despite overseas fab expansion costs, TSMC's Q4 2025 gross margin rose 330 basis points YoY to 62.3%, with Q1 2026 guidance between 63%-65%.
вҖў TSMC expects robust demand for advanced nodes and high factory utilization to help meet its 2026 gross margin targets.
вҖў Intel (INTC) is investing in its foundry business with the 18A process, aiming to compete with TSMC's upcoming N2 chips.
news.Keywords: Semiconductors, AI chips, manufacturing, gross margin, global expansion

news.Title: Lumentum Holdings (LITE) Climbs 8.8% on AI Optimism, Expansion
news.Content: Lumentum Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:LITE) is one of the 10 Stocks Outperforming Wall Street with Outsized Gains . Lumentum Holdings rallied for a second day on Wednesday, jumping 8.81 percent to ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Lumentum Holdings (LITE) rallied 8.81% on Wednesday, driven by AI optimism and its planned manufacturing expansion in the US.
вҖў Lumentum acquired a 240,000-square-foot facility from chipmaker Qorvo to produce InP optical devices for AI data centers.
вҖў Nvidia Corp. will be a customer of the facility, supporting US critical infrastructure and R&D.
вҖў Lumentum CEO Michael Hurlston stated the expansion will significantly increase capacity and deepen strategic partnerships for the AI revolution.
news.Keywords: AI, data center, optical devices, manufacturing expansion, Nvidia

news.Title: Goldman Sachs Initiates Qualcomm (QCOM) at Neutral, Notes Mixed Growth Outlook
news.Content: QUALCOMM Incorporated (NASDAQ: QCOM ) is included among the 14 Cheap DRIP Stocks to Buy Now . KДҒrlis DambrДҒns/Flickr On March 30, Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider initiated coverage of QUALCOMM Incorporated ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Goldman Sachs initiated coverage of QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) with a Neutral rating and $135 price target, citing diversification efforts offset by potential share losses at key smartphone customers like Apple.
вҖў Bernstein downgraded Qualcomm to Market Perform with a lower price target, citing memory-related pressures impacting smartphone shipments and potentially too high consensus estimates.
вҖў Qualcomm is working to diversify revenue into automotive, PCs, and data centers using its IP.
вҖў Potential expiration of the Apple license agreement within the next year poses a risk.
news.Keywords: Semiconductors, smartphone market, diversification, analyst rating, license agreement

еӨ–иіҮеҗҢжӯҘдёӢдҝ®д»Ҡе№ҙе…ЁзҗғжүӢж©ҹеҮәиІЁйҮҸпјҢй җдј°е№ҙжёӣ13%пјҢе…¶дёӯAndroidйҷЈзҮҹиЎ°йҖҖе№…еәҰйҒ”15%пјҢиҮіж–јiPhoneпјҢеүҮеӣ ж–°е“ҒзҷјиЎЁжҷӮзЁӢиӘҝж•ҙпјҢйғЁеҲҶж©ҹеһӢеҸҜиғҪз”ұд»Ҡе№ҙз§ӢеӯЈе»¶еҫҢиҮіжҳҺе№ҙжҳҘеӯЈжҺЁеҮәпјҢеҪұйҹҝе…Ёе№ҙеҮәиІЁиЎЁзҸҫгҖӮ

д»–дёҰеј·иӘҝпјҢAIдјәжңҚеҷЁиҲҮйӣІз«ҜжңҚеӢҷжҘӯиҖ…е°ҚиЁҳжҶ¶й«”еғ№ж јдёҠжјІжүҝеҸ—еҠӣијғй«ҳпјҢдҪҶж¶ҲиІ»з«Ҝе°Қеғ№ж јж•Ҹж„ҹеәҰжҳҺйЎҜдёҚеҗҢгҖӮеңЁдёӯжқұжҲ°дәӢиҲҮйҖҡиҶЁеЈ“еҠӣдәӨз№”дёӢпјҢж•ҙй«”зү©еғ№дёҠжјІи¶ЁеӢўе·ІйҖҗжӯҘж“ҙж•ЈпјҢеёӮе ҙдёҚе®ңйҒҺеәҰжңҹеҫ…fedйҷҚжҒҜпјҢгҖҢдёҚеҚҮжҒҜгҖҚжҒҗе·ІжҳҜе°ҚиӮЎеёӮжңҖжңүеҲ©зҡ„жғ…еўғгҖӮ

news.Content:
пјҲдёӢеҚҲ5жҷӮ16еҲҶжӣҙж–°еӨ–иіҮиІ·иіЈи¶…еҖӢиӮЎпјү

еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ1пјүж—ҘеӢҒжҸҡ1451.83й»һпјҢж”¶еңЁ33174.82й»һгҖӮдёүеӨ§жі•дәәиҒҜиўӮиІ·и¶…549.82е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮеӣһиЈң232.51е„„е…ғпјҢзөӮзөҗйҖЈзәҢ4иіЈпјҢдё»иҰҒиҪүиІ·и¶…дё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·пјҲ00919пјү6.6иҗ¬ејөпјӣиЁҳжҶ¶й«”иӮЎиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»пјҲ2344пјүе’ҢеҠӣз©Қйӣ»пјҲ6770пјүеүҮйҖҶеӢўйҒӯиӘҝзҜҖгҖӮеҲҶжһҗеё«иЎЁзӨәпјҢеӨ–иіҮиІ·и¶…йҮ‘йЎҚдёҚз®—еӨҡпјҢеғ…иғҪзЁұзӮәгҖҢиё©еүҺи»ҠгҖҚпјҢе…¶д»ҚеңЁи©•дј°еҫҢзәҢеұҖеӢўзҡ„зҷјеұ•пјҢе°ҡжңӘеҮәзҸҫжҳҺзўәзҡ„й•·з·ҡеӣһиЈңиЁҠиҷҹгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮд»Ҡж—ҘиІ·и¶…еүҚдёүеӨ§пјҡдё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·гҖҒеҸ°ж–°ж–°е…үйҮ‘гҖҒеҸӢйҒ”

дё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·пјҲ00919пјүд»Ҡе№ҙдҫҶиЎЁзҸҫжҗ¶зңјпјҢеҗёеј•иіҮйҮ‘еӨ§иҲүж№§е…ҘпјҢеӨ–иіҮд»ҠзөӮзөҗйҖЈ3иіЈпјҢиҪүиІ·и¶…6иҗ¬6,509ејөеұ…еҸ°иӮЎиІ·и¶…д№ӢеҶ пјӣдё»еӢ•еҫ©иҸҜжңӘдҫҶ50пјҲ00991Aпјүд№ҹжңү3иҗ¬904ејөиІ·и¶…гҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮдё»иҰҒиІ·и¶…йӢјйҗөеЎ‘еҢ–иӮЎпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬дёӯйӢјпјҲ2002пјү1иҗ¬8,820ејөгҖҒеҚ—дәһпјҲ1303пјү1иҗ¬6,451ејөд»ҘеҸҠеҸ°еҢ–пјҲ1326пјү9,229ејөгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮд»Ҡж—ҘиіЈи¶…еүҚдёүеӨ§пјҡиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»гҖҒеҠӣз©Қйӣ»гҖҒзҫӨеүө

жҶ¶й«”ж—ҸзҫӨд»Ҡж—ҘйҡЁи‘—зҫҺе…үжӯўи·ҢеҸҚеҪҲпјҢз„¶иҖҢпјҢеӨ–иіҮйҖҶеӢўз ҚиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»пјҲ2344пјү3иҗ¬628ејөпјҢд»ҘеҸҠеҠӣз©Қйӣ»пјҲ6770пјү2иҗ¬6,479ејөгҖӮзӣӨйқўдә®зңјзҡ„йқўжқҝж—ҸзҫӨеүҮжҳҜдёҚеҗҢиӘҝпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҮәи„«зҫӨеүөпјҲ3481пјү1иҗ¬3,926ејөпјҢиІ·и¶…еҸӢйҒ”пјҲ2409пјү3иҗ¬3,792ејөгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮеғ…иІ·233е„„е…ғ еҲҶжһҗеё«пјҡгҖҢиё©еүҺи»ҠгҖҚжңӘзҝ»еӨҡ

ж №ж“ҡиӯүдәӨжүҖе…¬еёғзұҢзўјеӢ•еҗ‘пјҢдёүеӨ§жі•дәәд»Ҡж—ҘеҗҲиЁҲиІ·и¶…549.82е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮиІ·и¶…232.51е„„е…ғпјҢзөӮзөҗйҖЈзәҢ4иіЈгҖӮеӨ–иіҮеүҚ4еҖӢдәӨжҳ“ж—Ҙе…ұиӘҝзҜҖеҸ°иӮЎйҒ”1886.58е„„е…ғгҖӮ

жҠ•дҝЎзәҢз«ҷиІ·ж–№пјҢиІ·и¶…101.1иҗ¬е…ғгҖӮиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶеҗҲиЁҲиІ·и¶…216.19е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯиҮӘиЎҢиІ·иіЈйғЁеҲҶиІ·и¶…67.6е„„е…ғпјҢйҒҝйҡӘж–№йқўиІ·и¶…148.58е„„е…ғгҖӮ

иӯүжңҹйӣҷз…§еҲҶжһҗеё«зҝҒеҒүжҚ·еҸ—иЁӘиЎЁзӨәпјҢеҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡж—ҘеӨ§жјІйҖҫеҚғй»һпјҢеҰӮжӯӨйү…еӨ§жјІе№…пјҢеӨ–иіҮз…§зҗҶжҮүиІ·и¶…дёғгҖҒе…«зҷҫе„„е…ғпјҢдҪҶд»Ҡж—ҘеӣһиЈңзҡ„йҮҸдёҚз®—еӨҡпјҢйЎҜзӨәе…¶е°ҚеҸ°иӮЎзҡ„еҫҢзәҢиЎЁзҸҫд»Қеӯҳжңүз–‘ж…®пјҢеғ…иғҪиҰ–зӮәзҹӯжңҹзҡ„гҖҢиё©еүҺи»ҠгҖҚпјҢиҖҢйқһе…ЁйқўзңӢеҘҪеӣһжӯёгҖӮ

зҝҒеҒүжҚ·иӘӘжҳҺпјҢеӨ–иіҮжҢҒи§Җжңӣж…ӢеәҰзҡ„дё»иҰҒеҺҹеӣ еңЁж–јиҚ·е§ҶиҢІжө·еіҪе°ҒйҺ–жҷӮй–“зҡ„й•·зҹӯпјҢе°Қе…Ёзҗғз¶“жҝҹжҢҒзәҢз”ўз”ҹеҪұйҹҝгҖӮжӯӨеӨ–пјҢж №ж“ҡзҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®жңҖж–°зҡ„иӘӘжі•пјҢжҲ°зҲӯеҸҜиғҪйӮ„йңҖиҮіе°‘е…©иҮідёүе‘ЁжүҚиғҪзөҗжқҹпјҢйҖҷдҪҝеҫ—еӨ–иіҮеңЁдҪңйӮҸијҜдёҠпјҢзӣ®еүҚеңЁе…ЁзҗғиіҮз”ўйғЁдҪҚд»ҚеӮҫеҗ‘д»ҘиіЈи¶…зӮәдё»пјҢе°ҚеҸ°иӮЎзҡ„иІ·зӣӨеҠӣйҒ“еӣ жӯӨеҸ—йҷҗгҖӮ

зҝҒеҒүжҚ·зәҢжҢҮпјҢеӨ–иіҮд»Ҡж—ҘеӢ•дҪңеҒҸеҗ‘пјҢеңЁзҹӯжңҹе…§е°ӢжүҫйҒҺеҺ»иЎЁзҸҫијғеј·еӢўзҡ„еҖӢиӮЎйҖІиЎҢеұҖйғЁеёғеұҖпјҢи§ҖеҜҹжҳҜеҗҰжңүзҹӯжңҹж©ҹжңғпјҢдёҰзӯүеҫ…жҷӮж©ҹеҶҚйҖІиЎҢеҫҢзәҢзҡ„еӨ§иҰҸжЁЎеӣһиЈңгҖӮ

зҺӢи©©е©·пјҸиІЎз¶“зү№жҙҫиЁҳиҖ…пјҢдё»и·‘еӢһеӢ•йғЁгҖҒеҚҠе°Һй«”гҖҒз”ҹжҠҖзӯү
